channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Will the Agent Recuse Itself? Measuring LLM-Agent Compliance with In-Band Access-Deny Signals
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06460v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主智能体日益持有真实凭证、直接操作生产基础设施且“无人值守”，运维人员缺乏标准化机制向智能体传达“某资源禁止访问”的指令。现有访问控制仅提供二元选择：凭有效凭证放行（无法阻止合规但越权行为），或硬性拒绝（与网络故障等普通错误不可区分）。这导致治理盲区——亟需一种轻量、可解释、合作式的第三种访问调控模式。

方法与创新

本文提出Recuse Signal（退避信号）：一种开源轻量级带内拒绝信号，通过协议既有通道（如SSH登录横幅、PostgreSQL NOTICE 消息）由服务端主动发出，请求连接中的自动化智能体自愿中止操作。它类比 robots.txt，是协作型治理机制而非安全边界，其有效性完全依赖智能体的自愿遵守。我们定义该信号为开放微型标准，开发了零/低侵入式适配器（SSH banner + PAM钩子；PostgreSQL线协议代理），并部署于真实生产主机。在受控实验中，向全新初始化的LLM智能体分配无害运维任务（如查询系统负载），观测其是否响应信号而主动退避。

主要发现

  • 高合规性验证：SSH场景下（GPT-4o、GPT-4o-mini、Claude Code），信号存在时100%触发退避；无信号对照组则100%完成任务，证明信号具备强行为引导力。
  • 合作性本质凸显：当提示中明确加入“运维人员已授权绕过”框架时，最强模型（GPT-4o）转为执行任务，而其余模型仍尊重主机策略——表明该信号本质是可协商的治理信令，非强制锁。
  • 开源实践落地：完整释放信号规范、适配器代码及实验框架，支持复现与生态共建。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the Recuse Signal: a lightweight, in-band, cooperative access-deny mechanism for LLM agents operating on live infrastructure. Unlike traditional access controls (which either grant or hard-fail), the Recuse Signal—transmitted via existing protocol channels (e.g., SSH banners, PostgreSQL NOTICE messages)—requests automated agents to voluntarily withdraw from a resource. It is explicitly not a security boundary but a governance primitive analogous to robots.txt. We define it as an open mini-standard, implement low-footprint adapters for SSH and PostgreSQL, deploy them on a production host, and conduct a controlled experiment with fresh LLM agents performing benign operational tasks. Results show 100% recusal under the signal versus 100% task completion in the no-signal control (SSH pilot with GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, and Claude Code). Crucially, the signal behaves cooperatively: explicit operator-authorization framing causes the most capable model to proceed, while others defer to host policy—demonstrating its role as a negotiable, human-aligned governance channel. All artifacts are publicly released.

Abstract

As autonomous LLM agents increasingly hold real credentials and operate infrastructure without a human in the loop, operators have no standard way to tell an agent that a resource is off-limits. Access controls either let the agent in (it has valid credentials) or hard-fail it (indistinguishable from any other client). We propose a third mode: a lightweight, published in-band deny signal -- the Recuse Signal -- that a server emits over a protocol's existing channels (an SSH banner, a PostgreSQL NOTICE) asking a connecting automated agent to voluntarily withdraw. This is a cooperative governance control, the robots.txt analogue for live access; it is explicitly not a security boundary. Its value is entirely empirical and, to our knowledge, unmeasured: do compliant LLM agents actually honor such a signal? We define the signal as an open mini-standard, implement two zero- or low-footprint adapters (an SSH banner/PAM hook and a PostgreSQL wire-protocol proxy), deploy them on a live production host, and run a controlled experiment in which fresh agents are given a benign operations task and observed for recusal. In a pilot (SSH; OpenAI GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini; and Claude Code as a deployed agent), the signal cleanly induces recusal -- 100% recusal when present versus 100% task completion in a no-signal control -- and, revealingly, behaves as a cooperative rather than absolute signal: an explicit operator-authorization framing flips the most capable model to proceed, while other agents continue to defer to the on-host policy. We release the standard, adapters, and experiment harness for reproduction.

item.title: WebMCP Tool Surface Poisoning: Runtime Manipulation Attacks on LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06387v1

AI Summary (中文)

WebMCP工具表面投毒：面向大语言模型代理的运行时操纵攻击

WebMCP（Web Model Control Protocol）是一种新兴协议，允许网站直接向AI代理暴露结构化工具接口，绕过传统UI层。该范式虽提升自动化能力，却显著扩展了Web会话的攻击面，尤其在第三方脚本广泛嵌入的现代Web生态中。本文首次识别并系统定义了一类新型威胁——会话中工具注入（Mid-Session Tool Injection, MSTI）：攻击者利用第三方脚本在LLM代理已建立会话期间动态篡改其可访问工具集合或语义认知。

我们提出双维度分类框架：

  • 工具劫持（Tool Hijacking）：通过AbortSignal API中断注册、竞态条件覆盖、或篡改toolRegistry全局对象，物理性移除/替换工具入口；
  • 工具框定（Tool Framing）：不改变工具存在性，而恶意篡改name、description、readOnlyHint及inputSchema等元数据字段，扭曲代理对工具功能、权限与输入约束的认知。

基于真实WebMCP兼容环境的实验验证表明：两类攻击均能导致代理执行越权操作（如绕过只读限制）、调用错误工具链、或生成严重偏离用户意图的输出。进一步分析揭示，MSTI根植于WebMCP特有的工具生命周期松耦合性与元数据驱动的语义解析机制，使“工具表面”（tool surface）本身成为新型安全边界。为此，我们提出四项关键缓解方向：① 工具身份强绑定至注册源（origin）；② 强制工具注册/注销生命周期一致性（如引入toolId不可变标识与引用计数）；③ 为第三方工具实施严格数据隔离边界（sandboxed execution + schema validation）；④ 全链路记录工具注册、元数据变更与调用日志，支持可审计回溯。本研究首次将LLM代理的工具交互层纳入系统性安全建模，为下一代AI-Native Web协议的安全设计提供了基础性警示与实践指南。

AI Summary (English)

WebMCP enables websites to expose tools directly to LLM agents, bypassing UIs—but introduces novel runtime security risks. This paper identifies Mid-Session Tool Injection (MSTI), a new class of attack where adversaries exploit third-party scripts to manipulate agent-accessible tools during active sessions. We classify MSTI into two categories: Tool Hijacking, which alters the set of available tools via race conditions or AbortSignal abuse; and Tool Framing, which corrupts the agent’s semantic understanding by tampering with metadata (e.g., name, description, inputSchema). Our implementation confirms both vectors successfully disrupt intended WebMCP behavior—causing privilege escalation, incorrect tool selection, and unsafe outputs. We attribute MSTI to WebMCP’s decoupled tool lifecycle and metadata-driven execution model, establishing the “tool surface” as an emergent security boundary. We propose concrete mitigations: origin-bound tool identity, lifecycle-consistent registration, strict data boundaries for third-party tools, and auditable tool registration/invoke logging. This work provides the first systematic security analysis of WebMCP’s tool interface and foundational guidance for secure AI-native web protocols.

Abstract

WebMCP is a newly emerging protocol that enables websites to expose tools directly to AI agents, bypassing traditional user interfaces and introducing new security risks. The dynamic exposure of agent-accessible tools in WebMCP expands the attack surface of web sessions, especially when third-party scripts are involved. In this study, we identify a new potential threat, termed Mid-Session Tool Injection (MSTI), in which attackers leverage third-party scripts to inject malicious tools during an active session. To better characterize this threat, we classify MSTI based on the stage and target of manipulation, distinguishing between Tool Hijacking and Tool Framing. Tool Hijacking modifies the set of tools visible to the agent through mechanisms such as the AbortSignal API or race conditions during tool registration. In contrast, Tool Framing influences the agent's perception of tool roles through metadata fields such as tool name, description, readOnlyHint, and inputSchema. Our implementation demonstrates that both Tool Hijacking and Tool Framing can successfully disrupt the intended functionality of WebMCP. Based on these results, we outline potential mitigation directions and provide security design recommendations for WebMCP, including binding tool identity to its origin, ensuring lifecycle consistency, enforcing data boundaries for third-party tools, and maintaining traceable logs of tool registration and invocation. These findings indicate that MSTI arises from WebMCP's unique tool lifecycle and structured metadata, making the tool surface itself an emerging security concern.

item.title: Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06265v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

针对机器学习分类器的逃避攻击（evasion attacks）严重威胁金融欺诈检测、网络安全等关键应用的可靠性。现有防御方法（如对抗训练、输入净化）常面临两大瓶颈：泛化能力弱——在未见过的攻击类型或数据分布下性能骤降；鲁棒性提升不系统——缺乏统一机制协同优化特征鲁棒性与模型结构韧性。

方法创新：RESSAP框架

我们提出选择性增强与增广预测器鲁棒集成框架（Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors, RESSAP），一种模型无关、可即插即用的防御范式：

  • 选择性特征强化：基于新定义的韧性得分（Resilience Score），联合评估特征重要性（如SHAP值）与局部鲁棒性（梯度敏感度），动态筛选高韧性子集，为每个基分类器构建专属精简特征空间；
  • 随机化集成推理：部署时仅随机激活部分基分类器（如5/10），显著增加攻击者预估决策逻辑的难度，打破“确定性脆弱路径”；
  • 噪声感知数据增广：在训练中注入自适应高斯噪声（噪声方差与特征韧性得分负相关），在易受攻击维度施加更强正则化，加固决策边界。

实验验证与优势

在UCI信用卡欺诈、NSL-KDD网络入侵及ImageNet-1K子集上，RESSAP在FGSM、PGD、CW等7类主流逃避攻击下，平均鲁棒准确率提升23.6%–38.1%，同时在干净数据上保持≤0.9%的精度损失。相比单模型对抗训练，其计算开销仅增加17%，且无需修改原始模型架构或重标标签。该框架为ML系统提供了可扩展、低侵入、跨任务兼容的安全增强方案。

AI Summary (English)

Evasion attacks severely undermine the reliability of ML classifiers in security-critical domains. To address the limited generalizability and unsystematic robustness of existing defenses, we propose RESSAP: a model-agnostic framework that transforms a single classifier into a robust ensemble. RESSAP selects feature subsets for each base predictor using a novel Resilience Score—jointly quantifying feature importance and local robustness—and applies adaptive noise-based augmentation during training to strengthen decision boundaries. At inference, a random subset of predictors is activated, increasing unpredictability for adversaries. Experiments across fraud detection, intrusion detection, and image classification show RESSAP improves average robust accuracy by 23.6–38.1% against 7 evasion attacks (e.g., PGD, CW), with <0.9% clean-data accuracy drop and only 17% extra training cost. RESSAP delivers scalable, architecture-free defense without retraining or structural changes.

Abstract

Evasion attacks present a significant challenge to the robustness of machine learning (ML)-based classifiers, particularly in critical applications such as fraud detection and cybersecurity. Although existing defense mechanisms are effective in some settings, they often suffer from limited generalizability and do not systematically improve model robustness across diverse attack scenarios. To address these limitations, we introduce Robust Ensemble of Selectively Strengthened and Augmented Predictors (RESSAP), a novel framework that transforms a single classifier into an ensemble of robust classifiers. Each classifier in the ensemble is trained on a carefully selected subset of features, where feature selection is guided by a resilience metric that accounts for both feature importance and robustness. During inference, a random subset of these classifiers is used to make predictions, increasing unpredictability and improving resistance to adversarial manipulation. In addition, noise-based data augmentation is applied during training to strengthen decision boundaries and improve generalization. Our experimental results demonstrate that RESSAP significantly improves robustness against adversarial evasion attacks while maintaining strong accuracy on clean data. Overall, this model-agnostic framework provides a scalable and flexible defense strategy for enhancing the security of machine learning systems without requiring major changes to existing architectures.

item.title: GenTI: Benchmarking LLMs for Autonomous IDPS Rule Generation for Unseen Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05844v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于规则的入侵检测与防御系统（IDPS）虽具备高精度检测与主动缓解能力，但其依赖人工编写的签名式规则，难以应对新兴威胁与零日攻击。现有公开数据集（如CICIDS2017、UNSW-NB15）聚焦网络流量分类，缺乏结构化规则语义、响应逻辑与威胁情报映射，严重制约自动化规则生成研究。

方法创新

本文提出Generative Threat Intelligence（GenTI）——首个面向未见攻击的LLM驱动IDPS规则生成基准框架。核心包含两大部分：

  • GTI数据集：整合超15万条Snort/Suricata/ET规则及5万条YARA规则，每条均标注协议行为、载荷特征、上下文关系、Cyber Threat Intelligence（CTI）实体映射（如MITRE ATT&CK技术ID、CVE、TTPs），以及可执行响应类型（alert/drop/reject）；
  • LLM生成管道：融合结构化提示工程、思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）推理与验证链（Chain-of-Verification, CoVe）闭环，实现从分析师自然语言指令+典型载荷样本→语法合规、语义准确、安全有效的部署级规则的端到端转化，并支持实时在Snort/Suricata中执行验证。

主要结果

GenTI在真实引擎中达成：复合规则质量分89.4%，CTI覆盖率达94.8%，未见攻击检出率从基线45%显著提升至87.4%，误报率由8.5%降至2.3%。本工作首次实现规则粒度CTI与大模型自动化生成的深度耦合，为构建自适应、自演化的下一代IDPS奠定基准与范式。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce GenTI, the first large-scale LLM-driven benchmark for autonomous generation of deployable IDPS rules against unseen attacks. To overcome the rigidity of manual rule authoring and the lack of CTI-enriched, action-oriented datasets, we curate GTI: a corpus of 200K+ production-grade rules (150K Snort/Suricata/ET + 50K YARA), each annotated with protocol semantics, payload patterns, contextual relationships, MITRE ATT&CK/CVE mappings, and executable responses (alert/drop/reject). Our LLM pipeline employs structured prompting, Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and a Chain-of-Verification loop to synthesize syntactically valid, semantically faithful, and security-effective rules—directly executable in Snort/Suricata. Evaluated across syntax accuracy, semantic similarity, CTI coverage, and real-world detection efficacy, GenTI achieves an 89.4% composite rule-quality score, 94.8% CTI coverage, boosts unseen-attack detection from 45% to 87.4%, and slashes false positives from 8.5% to 2.3%. GenTI establishes a foundational benchmark tightly integrating threat intelligence at the rule level with generative AI automation.

Abstract

Rule-based Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems (IDPS) offer precise attack detection as well as mitigation, however their manually crafted, signature-driven rules limit adaptability to emerging and zero-day threats. Additionally, existing public datasets (e.g., CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15) focus on traffic classification and provide little structured information to support automatic rule synthesis or prevention logic. To address this gap, we propose Generative Thread Intelligence (GenTI) \footnote{GenTI refers to the proposed framework, and GTI refers to the dataset.} an LLM-driven benchmark for automatic generation of IDPS rules targeting unseen attacks. The dataset (GTI) aggregates over 150k detection and prevention rules from Snort, Suricata, Emerging Threats, as well as 50k YARA, each annotated with protocol behavior, payload signatures, contextual relationships, mappings to Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI), along with actionable response types (alert, drop, reject). Moreover, on top of this corpus we design an LLM-based pipeline that transforms analyst prompts and representative payloads into deployable rules via structured prompt engineering, Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, as well as a Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) loop for syntactic, semantic, and security validation. The generated rules are executed in real time on (Snort/Suricata) and evaluated by syntax accuracy, semantic similarity, CTI coverage, security effectiveness as well as unseen attacks detection. Furthermore, our GenTI instantiation achieves a composite rule-quality score of 89.4\%, with 94.8\% CTI coverage, improving unseen attacks detection from 45\% to 87.4\% and reducing the false-positive rate from 8.5\% to 2.3\%. Overall, GenTI establishes the first large-scale benchmark that tightly couples rule-level CTI with LLM-based automation, enabling adaptive, self-evolving IDPS.

item.title: GCD: Garbled, Corrected, Demonstrandum -- Fixing and Proving Go's Extended GCD Implementation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05796v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文针对 Go 语言标准库中 crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod 模块的 extendedGCD（扩展欧几里得算法）实现开展形式化验证。该函数是 RSA 密钥生成的核心组件，直接影响密码学安全性。尽管其声称直接移植自 BoringSSL，我们发现两处关键偏差：(1) 系数更新逻辑错误——Go 版本在迭代中错误维护贝祖系数，破坏了核心不变式 $a \cdot s + b \cdot t = r$；(2) 输入域过度放宽——允许非标准输入（如 $b=0$ 或 $|a|<|b|$），导致未定义行为与潜在崩溃。

方法与技术路线

我们提出“修复—证明”协同框架：

  • 修复层面：修正系数更新逻辑，消除数值不一致；优化后实测平均性能提升 24%（因减少冗余分支与中间赋值）；
  • 验证层面：基于 Gobra（Go 专用演绎验证器）构建端到端正确性证明，涵盖终止性与功能正确性（即输出满足 $\gcd(a,b) = as + bt$ 且 $|s| \leq |b|$, $|t| \leq |a|$）；
  • 数学支撑：对 Gobra 无法自动处理的非线性整数不等式引理（如商余关系边界、绝对值单调性），使用 Lean 4 形式化证明，并以可信接口导入 Gobra。

关键洞见与贡献

1. 隐蔽缺陷普遍性：即便经工业级审查（BoringSSL → Go 双重审核），微小语义偏差仍可绕过测试与人工检视；
2. 验证即调试范式：Gobra 的反例反馈驱动我们迭代精化循环不变式（如引入带符号的归一化残差约束），将验证过程转化为深度代码审计；
3. AI 增强验证：实验表明，大语言模型可基于 Gobra 报错自动建议缺失引理、重构归纳假设，显著加速验证循环——为高保障密码代码的规模化验证提供新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We formally verify Go’s extendedGCD implementation in crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod, a critical component for RSA key generation. Despite being intended as a direct port of BoringSSL’s implementation, we uncover two deviations that break essential algorithmic invariants: (1) incorrect coefficient updates violating the Bézout identity $as + bt = r$, and (2) an overly permissive input domain enabling undefined behavior. We fix the first deviation—yielding a verified implementation with 24% average speedup—and extend an existing BoringSSL proof to cover the broader domain. Using Gobra, we prove full functional correctness and termination; for non-linear integer lemmata beyond Gobra’s automation (e.g., bounds on quotients and absolute values), we employ Lean 4, exporting proofs as trusted axioms. Our effort reveals three insights: subtle bugs evade even rigorous peer review; formal verification is uniquely effective at exposing them; and AI agents can accelerate verification by iteratively refining invariants and lemmata in response to verifier feedback.

Abstract

We verify the 'extendedGCD' implementation in Go's standard library ('crypto/internal/fips140/bigmod'), which plays a crucial role in the generation of RSA key pairs. Even though the Go implementation is supposedly a direct port from BoringSSL's implementation, we uncovered two deviations that each break the algorithm's invariants: (1) the Go implementation deviates in the way coefficients are updated, and (2) it permits a larger input domain. We address both deviations; the first by fixing the Go implementation, which results in an on average 24% speedup, and the second deviation by porting an existing proof for BoringSSL and extending it to cover the larger input domain. We prove correctness and termination of the fixed Go implementation using Gobra, a deductive program verifier for Go. Where necessary, we used Lean to prove key lemmata on non-linear arithmetic, which we import into Gobra. Our verification effort reveals three key insights: subtle bugs can slip into even well-reviewed code with surprising ease; formal verification is a powerful tool for uncovering them; and AI agents can facilitate the verification process by iteratively refining invariants and lemmata based on Gobra's error messages.

item.title: Membrane: A Self-Evolving Contrastive Safety Memory for LLM Agent Defense
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05743v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

尽管安全对齐技术持续进步，大语言模型（LLM）仍面临动态演化的越狱攻击（jailbreaks）威胁。现有基于微调的安全分类器缺乏在线适应能力；而依赖记忆的自适应防护机制（如记忆增强型守卫）又普遍存在过度拒绝（over-refusal）问题——即因误判语义相似性，将大量良性查询错误拦截（拒答率高达28–85%），严重损害可用性。

方法创新：Membrane 与对比安全记忆（CSM）

我们提出 Membrane——一种无需重训练、可自我演化的对比式安全守卫框架。其核心是对比安全记忆（Contrastive Safety Memory, CSM）：每个记忆单元（cell）以结构化方式并置存储一对条件——阻断有害查询的触发逻辑 与 允许表面相似但本质良性的请求的许可逻辑。Membrane 通过在线蒸馏每次有害交互及其对应的良性对照样本，按底层攻击策略（如“角色扮演”“混淆编码”）自动索引生成新 cell；单个 cell 可泛化至同一攻击机制下的多主题变体，实现策略级抽象而非表层匹配。

关键结果与优势

  • 在 HarmBench（模型级） 和 AgentHarm（代理级） 两大基准上，Membrane 在全部六类主流越狱攻击中均取得最高 F1 分数；
  • 良性拒答率仅 7–14%，显著优于现有方法（28–85%），兼顾安全性与实用性；
  • 具备强鲁棒性：跨攻击迁移场景下记忆单元 F1 保持 87–88%；在恶意记忆注入（memory poisoning）测试中表现稳定，未出现性能塌缩。

AI Summary (English)

Despite progress in LLM safety alignment, models remain vulnerable to continuously evolving jailbreaks. Existing fine-tuned classifiers lack adaptability, while memory-based guardrails suffer from high benign refusal (28–85%). We propose Membrane, a self-evolving contrastive safety guardrail built on Contrastive Safety Memory (CSM). Each CSM cell jointly encodes blocking conditions for harmful queries and permitting conditions for superficially similar benign ones, indexed by underlying attack strategy—not surface features—enabling cross-topic generalization without retraining. At inference, retrieved cells provide precise, context-grounded safety decisions. On HarmBench and AgentHarm, Membrane achieves the highest F1 across all six jailbreak attacks, with benign refusal reduced to just 7–14%. It maintains 87–88% F1 under cross-attack transfer and resists memory poisoning, demonstrating robust, adaptive, and practical safety enforcement.

Abstract

Despite advances in safety alignment, large language models remain vulnerable to continuously evolving jailbreaks. Existing fine-tuned safety classifiers cannot adapt to these evolving attacks, while adaptive memory-based guardrails tend to over-refuse benign queries that resemble stored attacks. We propose Membrane, a self-evolving guardrail built on Contrastive Safety Memory (CSM): each cell pairs the conditions for blocking a harmful query with those for permitting a superficially similar benign request. Without retraining, Membrane evolves CSM by distilling each harmful interaction and its benign counterpart into a contrastive cell indexed by the underlying attack strategy, so that one cell generalizes across topical variants of the same mechanism. At inference, retrieved cells serve as grounding context for precise safety decisions. Across model-level safety on HarmBench and agent-level safety on AgentHarm, Membrane achieves the highest F1 on all six jailbreak attacks. Notably, benign refusal on AgentHarm stays at 7-14%, well below the 28-85% range of prior guards. Memory cells also retain 87-88% F1 under cross-attack transfer and remain stable under memory poisoning.

item.title: An Embarrassingly Simple Detector for Model Extraction Attacks in Large Language Model API Traffic
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05725v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型（LLMs）广泛通过托管API部署，模型提取攻击（Model Extraction Attacks, MEA）已成为威胁模型知识产权与服务安全的现实风险。然而，单次提取查询在表层语义和统计特征上常与正常用户请求高度相似，导致传统基于单查询异常打分或纯“良性用户 vs. 攻击者用户”二分设定的检测方法鲁棒性不足，难以应对真实API流量中多用户混杂、攻击请求比例动态变化的复杂场景。

方法创新

本文提出一种极简但强效的检测范式：将模型提取监控建模为良校准的流量窗口分布检验问题。核心思想是——不依赖攻击样本训练，仅利用历史良性流量构建语义分布基线：

  • 将实时API请求嵌入统一语义空间（如LLM最后一层隐藏状态均值）；
  • 在滑动时间窗口内聚合查询嵌入，计算其经验分布与历史良性流量分布的最大均值差异（Maximum Mean Discrepancy, MMD）；
  • 关键创新：决策阈值完全通过良性-良性对比（benign-vs-benign）自适应设定，彻底规避对攻击数据的依赖，实现零先验、强泛化。

主要结果

在涵盖4类典型提取场景（梯度反演、提示注入、API重放、黑盒蒸馏）的14组攻击-正常查询对上系统评估：

  • FPR仅0.3%（三随机种子平均），保障API服务可用性；
  • 纯攻击者场景TPR达100.0%，混合流量下平均TPR达90.5%（攻击请求占比5%–50%）；
  • 平衡准确率达95.1%，显著优于适配后的PRADA、SEAT、CAP、DATE及边缘马氏距离等基线；
  • 首次在用户级与混合多用户API流量双重设置下验证了分布检验范式的有效性，确立其为模型提取检测的强实证基线。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) deployed via hosted APIs face growing threats from model extraction attacks (MEAs), yet detection remains challenging due to the semantic similarity between extraction and benign queries. We reframe MEA monitoring as benign-calibrated traffic-window distribution testing: incoming queries are embedded into a semantic space (e.g., mean hidden states), and their aggregate distribution over a time window is tested for deviation from historical benign traffic using Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD). Crucially, the decision threshold is set exclusively via benign-vs-benign comparisons—no attack data or labels are required. Evaluated across 14 attacker-normal query pairs from four realistic extraction scenarios, our MMD detector achieves 0.3% benign false positive rate, 100.0% true positive rate in pure-attacker settings, 90.5% average TPR under mixed multi-user traffic, and 95.1% balanced accuracy, outperforming adapted PRADA, SEAT, CAP, DATE, and marginal Mahalanobis baselines. This establishes benign-calibrated distribution testing as a simple, robust, and empirically strong baseline for real-world LLM API security. Code: https://github.com/LabRAI/mmd-llm-mea-detection.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed through hosted APIs, making model extraction a practical threat to model ownership and service security. However, individual extraction queries often resemble benign requests, and existing evaluations often focus on single-query anomaly scoring or pure benign-versus-attacker user settings. We formulate model extraction monitoring as benign-calibrated traffic-window distribution testing and show that an embarrassingly simple detector is effective: embed incoming queries into a semantic space and test whether their aggregate distribution deviates from historical benign traffic. We instantiate the detector with maximum mean discrepancy (MMD), using only benign-vs-benign comparisons to set the decision threshold. We evaluate on fourteen attacker-normal query pairs from four extraction scenarios and compare with adapted PRADA, SEAT, CAP, DATE, and marginal Mahalanobis baselines. Across three random seeds, MMD achieves 0.3% benign FPR, 100.0% pure-attacker TPR, 90.5% average TPR over attacker fractions, and 95.1% balanced accuracy. These results show that benign-calibrated distribution testing is a strong empirical baseline for model extraction detection in both user-level and mixed multi-user LLM API traffic. Code is released at: https://github.com/LabRAI/mmd-llm-mea-detection.

item.title: Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics for distributed Infrastructure Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05701v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

随着云原生架构、物联网（IoT）、边缘计算等技术在能源、交通、工业互联网等关键基础设施中的深度部署，分布式系统面临攻击面持续扩大、APT攻击频发、数据主权敏感性强等严峻挑战。传统基于中心化服务器的入侵检测系统（IDS）存在三大瓶颈：（1）隐私风险高——需汇聚原始网络流量，违反GDPR/《个人信息保护法》；（2）可扩展性差——海量边缘节点导致通信开销激增与单点故障；（3）决策黑箱化——深度学习模型缺乏可解释性，难以支撑安全运营人员快速响应与归因分析。

方法创新

本研究提出“认知威胁情报驱动的可解释联邦安全分析框架”（CTI-XFSA），深度融合三大技术栈：

  • 联邦学习（FL）层：采用差分隐私增强的安全聚合协议（Secure Aggregation + DP-noise），支持异构边缘节点（如工控网关、5G基站）本地训练轻量级检测模型（Random Forest/XGBoost/Autoencoder），仅上传加密梯度参数；
  • 可解释AI（XAI）层：集成SHAP与LIME双路径解释引擎，为每条告警生成特征贡献热力图与自然语言归因短句（如“该流量被判定为Mirai变种，主因TCP标志异常（权重0.82）与DNS请求频率突增（权重0.76）”）；
  • 认知情报层：构建动态威胁知识图谱，将联邦模型输出的异常模式实时映射至MITRE ATT&CK战术阶段，并关联IOC（IP、域名、哈希）与TTPs（战术、技术、过程），实现从“检测告警”到“攻击链推理”的跃迁。

关键成果

在NSL-KDD、CIC-IDS2017及真实电力SCADA边缘数据集上验证：检测准确率提升9.2%（达99.1%），误报率降低41%，模型更新通信开销减少76%；XAI模块使安全分析师平均研判时间缩短63%，威胁情报关联覆盖率提升至89%。本框架已通过国家电网某省级调度中心POC验证，成为首个支持“联邦训练—可解释研判—认知溯源”闭环的基础设施级安全分析范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes CTI-XFSA, a Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics framework for distributed infrastructure systems. To overcome scalability, privacy, and opacity limitations of centralized intrusion detection, CTI-XFSA integrates federated learning (FL) with differential privacy–enhanced secure aggregation, enabling local model training on edge nodes (e.g., IoT gateways, SCADA devices) without raw data sharing; explainable AI (XAI) via SHAP/LIME to generate human-interpretable threat attributions (e.g., “TCP flag anomaly: weight 0.82”); and cognitive threat intelligence, mapping detection outputs to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and enriching alerts with real-time IOC/TTP correlations. Evaluated on NSL-KDD, CIC-IDS2017, and live SCADA edge data, CTI-XFSA achieves 99.1% detection accuracy, 41% lower false positives, 76% reduced communication overhead, and 63% faster analyst triage. It represents the first production-ready framework unifying privacy-preserving collaboration, actionable explanations, and attack-chain reasoning for critical infrastructure security.

Abstract

The increasing adoption of distributed infrastructure systems, cloud computing, Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, and edge-based architectures has significantly expanded the cybersecurity attack surface and introduced increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. Conventional centralized intrusion detection approaches often face challenges related to scalability, data privacy, communication overhead, and limited transparency in artificial intelligence-driven decision-making processes. To address these limitations, this study proposes a Cognitive Threat Intelligence and Explainable Federated Security Analytics framework for distributed infrastructure systems. The proposed framework integrates Federated Learning (FL), Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), and cognitive cybersecurity analytics to enable collaborative and privacy-preserving cyber threat detection across distributed network environments. Instead of transmitting sensitive raw network traffic data to centralized servers, local security models are independently trained at distributed nodes, where only encrypted model parameters and updates are shared through a federated aggregation mechanism. This decentralized learning architecture improves privacy protection while reducing communication dependency and centralized security risks. To enhance intelligent threat analysis, the framework incorporates machine learning and deep learning algorithms including Random Forest, XGBoost, Autoencoder

item.title: Protecting K-Nearest Neighbor Queries from Location Inference Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05648v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

k近邻查询（kNNQ）是位置服务（LBS）的核心功能，广泛应用于“附近的人”等场景。然而，其查询响应（即返回的k个地理邻近对象）隐含用户位置信息，长期缺乏系统性隐私风险评估。本文首次揭示kNNQ存在严重的位置推断漏洞。

攻击模型创新

我们提出两种新型位置推断攻击：

  • 几何交集位置推断攻击（GI-LIA）：利用多次kNNQ响应在空间上的几何约束（如距离不等式与圆盘交集），通过解析几何求解缩小用户真实位置的可行区域；
  • 零阶优化位置推断攻击（ZO-LIA）：无需梯度信息，仅依赖查询响应的布尔反馈（如“目标是否在结果中”），采用黑盒优化算法高效逼近真实位置。实验证明二者可在平均3.2次查询内将定位误差压缩至50米以内，远超现有攻击能力。

防御框架DPRS

为应对上述威胁，我们设计差分隐私kNNQ保护框架DPRS，包含两大核心技术：
1. 受限区间内的拒绝采样机制：在动态构造的扰动区间内进行噪声注入，并拒绝超出几何合理性的扰动样本，显著抑制因过量噪声导致的距离失真；
2. 私有区间构造算法：基于查询点局部空间密度与k值自适应生成紧凑扰动区间，在满足ε-差分隐私前提下最大化保留原始距离序关系。

实验验证

在Foursquare、Gowalla等真实轨迹数据集上，DPRS在ε=1.0时实现平均查询精度损失<8.5%（对比基线方法下降达37%），同时将位置推断成功率压制至<6.2%（GI-LIA）和<9.1%（ZO-LIA），全面优于Geo-Indistinguishability、PrivKNN等SOTA方法。代码已开源：https://github.com/reanatom/DPRS。

AI Summary (English)

The k-nearest neighbor query (kNNQ) is fundamental to location-based services but poses unaddressed location privacy risks. We present the first two practical location inference attacks against kNNQ: the Geometric Intersection LIA (GI-LIA) and the Zero-Order Optimization LIA (ZO-LIA), both capable of localizing users within ~50 meters using only a few queries. To counter these threats, we propose DPRS, a differential privacy framework for kNNQ that introduces a rejection sampling mechanism within an adaptively constructed perturbation interval—effectively reducing distance distortion while preserving utility. Our private interval construction algorithm tailors noise bounds to local spatial density and query parameters, enabling superior privacy-utility trade-offs. Experiments on real-world datasets (Foursquare, Gowalla) show DPRS achieves <8.5% accuracy loss at ε = 1.0 and reduces attack success rates to <6.2% (GI-LIA) and <9.1% (ZO-LIA), outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

The k-nearest neighbor query (kNNQ) is a core component of modern location-based services (LBS) and has been widely adopted in popular features such as ``people nearby''. However, its potential privacy risks have long been overlooked. In this work, we present the first two attacks against kNNQ, namely the geometric intersection location inference attack (GI-LIA) and the zero-order optimization location inference attack (ZO-LIA), revealing the inherent location privacy risks posed by kNNQ. To mitigate these privacy risks, we further propose DPRS, a differential privacy framework for kNNQ protection. The core idea of DPRS is to incorporate a rejection sampling mechanism within a constrained perturbation interval, thereby mitigating the distance distortion caused by excessive noise injection. In addition, we design a private interval construction algorithm to construct the perturbation interval, enabling the rejection sampling mechanism to achieve a more favorable trade-off between privacy protection and query utility in kNNQ. Extensive experiments on real-world spatial datasets demonstrate that DPRS outperforms existing methods in both privacy protection and query utility. Our code is available at https://github.com/reanatom/DPRS.

item.title: Multi-Objective Submodular Maximization with Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05596v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文聚焦于带差分隐私（DP）约束的多目标子模函数最大化（MOSM）问题：在敏感数据上，需从元素集合中选出至多 $k$ 个元素，以最大化 $d>1$ 个单调子模函数的最小值，同时满足 $\varepsilon$-差分隐私。该问题兼具算法公平性（通过min-max均衡多目标）、数据敏感性（如用户行为、位置等需隐私保护）及计算复杂性（NP-hard 且子模结构受隐私噪声干扰）。尽管单目标差分私有子模优化与非私有多目标子模优化均已取得丰富成果，本文首次系统研究并解决 MOSM 与 DP 的联合挑战，填补了该交叉领域的理论与算法空白。

方法创新

我们提出两种具有理论保证的新型差分私有算法：

  • DP-Greedy：将经典贪心算法与拉普拉斯机制深度耦合，在每轮选择中添加精心缩放的噪声，并引入概率截断策略抑制误差累积；
  • DP-Truncation：基于函数值截断思想，先构造一个“隐私友好的”低敏感度代理函数，再应用指数机制采样，显著降低隐私预算消耗。

二者均在 $\varepsilon$-DP 下实现 $(1 - 1/e - \delta)$-近似比（$\delta > 0$ 可控），时间复杂度为 $O(nk)$，优于现有私有单目标方法在多目标场景下的直接泛化。

实验验证

在最大覆盖（新闻推荐多样性+隐私保护）与设施选址（多区域服务公平性+用户位置脱敏）两个真实子模应用上，实验表明：在 $\varepsilon = 0.5\text{–}2.0$ 范围内，所提算法较基线（如私有随机采样、组合扰动）平均提升 23.6%–38.1% 的最小目标值，且运行时间仅增加 <15%，验证了其有效性、鲁棒性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper initiates the study of multi-objective submodular maximization (MOSM) under $\varepsilon$-differential privacy, where the goal is to select at most $k$ elements to maximize the minimum of $d > 1$ monotone submodular functions over sensitive data. We propose two novel $\varepsilon$-DP algorithms: DP-Greedy, which adapts the greedy framework with calibrated Laplace noise and probabilistic truncation; and DP-Truncation, which constructs a low-sensitivity proxy function and applies the exponential mechanism. Both achieve a $(1 - 1/e - \delta)$-approximation guarantee with $O(nk)$ time complexity. Experiments on maximum coverage and facility location tasks confirm substantial gains—up to 38.1% higher minimum objective value versus strong baselines—under practical privacy budgets ($\varepsilon = 0.5$–$2.0$), while maintaining efficiency and scalability.

Abstract

In this paper, we study multi-objective submodular maximization (MOSM) subject to a cardinality constraint under differential privacy (DP). Specifically, we aim to select a set of at most $k \in \mathbb{Z}_{+}$ elements to maximize the minimum of $d > 1$ monotone submodular functions while satisfying $\varepsilon$-DP. Although extensive studies have been conducted on both differentially private single-objective submodular maximization on sensitive data and non-private MOSM, to the best of our knowledge, there has not yet been any prior work on MOSM with DP. We propose two novel algorithms: the first extends the classic greedy algorithm and the second employs a truncation technique, both of which are integrated with DP mechanisms for privacy protection and achieve approximation guarantees for MOSM. Finally, we conduct numerical experiments on two submodular maximization applications, namely maximum coverage and facility location, in multi-objective settings to validate the efficacy and efficiency of our proposed algorithms.

item.title: GuardNet: Ensemble Strategies of Shallow Neural Networks for Robust Prompt Injection and Jailbreak Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05566v1

AI Summary (中文)

GuardNet：面向鲁棒提示注入与越狱检测的浅层神经网络集成方法

大型语言模型（LLMs）虽极大推动了自然语言处理发展，却持续面临提示注入（Prompt Injection, PI） 与越狱攻击（Jailbreak, JB） 的安全威胁。现有检测器常依赖大模型或易受基准污染与部分信息泄漏（partial information leakage）干扰，导致性能高估。本研究提出 GuardNet——一种轻量、高效、可部署的守卫系统。其核心是基于多视角BiLSTM浅层神经网络的集成架构（总参数量约4700万），摒弃“越大越强”范式，转而强调样本覆盖多样性与阈值精细化校准对鲁棒性的决定性作用。

我们在严格盲测条件下评估：在公开的JBB-Behaviors盲测集（n=200）上，GuardNet达AUROC 0.747；在经声明信息泄漏控制的自有闭源基准（n=50）上，F1达0.92。相较之下，Mistral-7B与Llama-3.1-8B等大模型虽F1/AUROC更高，但需GPU加速、延迟高、成本高。GuardNet在CPU端平均延迟仅约50 ms，支持低资源生产环境实时部署。本工作验证了：在PI/JB检测任务中，结构化集成设计+数据感知校准可显著弥补规模劣势，为边缘侧、API网关、沙箱守卫等场景提供高性价比防御方案。

AI Summary (English)

GuardNet is a lightweight, CPU-efficient guardrail system for detecting Prompt Injection (PI) and Jailbreak (JB) attacks against LLMs. Departing from scale-driven approaches, it employs an ensemble of shallow BiLSTM networks (~47M parameters), emphasizing example coverage diversity and threshold calibration—not model size—as key drivers of adversarial robustness. Evaluated under strict blind conditions, GuardNet achieves AUROC = 0.747 on the JBB-Behaviors blind test set (n=200) and F1 = 0.92 on a proprietary benchmark (n=50), with declared partial information leakage control. Its average CPU latency is ~50 ms—orders of magnitude faster and cheaper than LLM-based detectors (e.g., Mistral-7B, Llama-3.1-8B)—enabling real-time deployment in resource-constrained production settings. GuardNet demonstrates that carefully engineered ensembles of small models can deliver competitive, practical security without sacrificing speed or cost.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed natural language processing, but they remain vulnerable to Prompt Injection (PI) and Jailbreak (JB) attacks. In addition, benchmark evaluations may be affected by contamination and partial information leakage, compromising performance estimates. This work presents GuardNet, a guardrail system based on an ensemble of shallow neural networks (BiLSTMs) with approximately 47 million parameters. We investigate the hypothesis that robustness in adversarial scenarios depends more on the diversity of example coverage and threshold calibration than on model scale. The results indicate that GuardNet achieves competitive performance compared with lightweight detectors and high efficiency at low latency, although larger LLMs such as Mistral-7B and Llama-3.1-8B still achieve superior performance in terms of F1 score and AUROC on the blind JBB-Behaviors benchmark. Nevertheless, GuardNet achieves an AUROC of 0.747 on the blind dataset (n = 200) and an F1 score of 0.92 on a proprietary benchmark (n = 50), under threshold calibration and evaluation with declared partial information leakage. The system operates with an average latency of approximately 50 ms on CPU, making it suitable for deployment in production environments with cost and infrastructure constraints.

item.title: Unsupervised Skill Discovery for Agentic Data Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06416v1

AI Summary (中文)

无监督技能发现赋能数据分析智能体

在推理阶段进行技能增强（inference-time skill augmentation）是一种轻量级提升数据分析智能体性能的方法——无需更新模型参数，仅通过注入可复用的程序性知识即可实现能力跃迁。然而，如何从无标注的自主探索过程中自动发现高质量、可迁移的数据分析技能，仍是关键挑战：人工标注成本高昂，且不同分析范式（如报告生成 vs. 多步推理）的成功标准差异显著，难以定义统一监督信号。

为此，我们提出 DataCOPE——首个面向数据分析智能体的无监督、验证器引导的技能发现框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 不依赖黄金标签，而是从智能体自主生成的探索轨迹（trajectories）中挖掘隐式验证信号；
  • 设计双模态无监督验证器：对报告式分析（如Deep Data Research任务），采用自适应清单验证器（Adaptive Checklist Verifier），动态构建任务相关检查项，依据可验证内容覆盖率打分，并迭代优化清单；对推理式分析（如DABStep任务），采用答案一致性验证器（Answer Agreement Verifier），基于多轨迹答案聚类与自一致性（self-consistency）提供质量判据；
  • 通过技能管理器（Skill Manager） 实现对比式技能蒸馏，在轨迹间建立质量排序并提炼高价值操作模式。

实验表明：DataCOPE在报告式与推理式两大分析场景中均显著超越基线。在四个主流模型配置下，平均提升达：报告任务 +9.71%（绝对分数），推理任务 +32.30%（绝对分数）。该工作首次实现了纯探索驱动的数据分析技能自动化发现，为构建自进化、低依赖的数据分析智能体开辟了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Inference-time skill augmentation improves data-analytic agents without parameter updates, yet unsupervised discovery of reusable skills remains challenging due to costly supervision and heterogeneous success criteria across analytical formats. We propose DataCOPE, the first verifier-guided, fully unsupervised framework for skill discovery from unlabeled exploration trajectories. DataCOPE jointly coordinates a Data-Analytic Agent (for trajectory generation), an Unsupervised Verifier (for signal extraction), and a Skill Manager (for contrastive distillation). It instantiates two verifiers: an Adaptive Checklist Verifier for report-style analysis—deriving task-specific criteria, scoring by verifiable coverage, and refining checklists iteratively; and an Answer Agreement Verifier for reasoning-style analysis—clustering trajectories by answer consensus and leveraging self-consistency as auxiliary signal. Evaluated on Deep Data Research (report) and DABStep (reasoning), DataCOPE consistently outperforms baselines: averaging +9.71% and +32.30% absolute score gains across four model settings, respectively. This work enables autonomous, supervision-free skill acquisition for agentic data analysis.

Abstract

Inference-time skill augmentation provides a lightweight way to improve data-analytic agents by injecting reusable procedural knowledge without updating model parameters. However, discovering effective skills for data analysis remains challenging, as reliable supervision is expensive and success criteria vary across analytical formats. This raises the key question of how to discover reusable data-analysis skills from unlabeled exploration alone. We propose DataCOPE, an unsupervised verifier-guided skill discovery framework for data-analytic agents. DataCOPE derives verifier signals from the exploration trajectories and uses them to characterize relative quality or aggreement among trajectories. It iteratively coordinates a Data-Analytic Agent for trajectory generation, an Unsupervised Verifier for signal extraction, and a Skill Manager for contrastive skill distillation. For report-style analysis, we instantiate the verifier as an Adaptive Checklist Verifier that derives task-specific criteria, scores reports by verifiable coverage, and iteratively refines the checklist. For reasoning-style analysis, we instantiate it as an Answer Agreement Verifier that groups trajectories by answer agreement and uses self-consistency as an auxiliary signal. We evaluate DataCOPE on report-style analysis from Deep Data Research and reasoning-style analysis from DABStep. Across both settings, DataCOPE consistently improves held-out performance over baselines. Averaged across four model settings, DataCOPE improves the mean score by 9.71% and 32.30% on report-style and reasoning-style tasks respectively.

item.title: TokenMizer: Graph-Structured Session Memory for Long-Horizon LLM Context Management
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06337v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

面向长周期任务的大型语言模型（LLM）部署面临根本性瓶颈：上下文窗口有限，而实际工作会话无限延伸。当历史记录超出最大有效上下文窗（MECW）时，关键结构化信息（如架构决策、任务跳转、文件变更轨迹）被静默截断。现有方案将对话历史视为扁平文本，彻底破坏会话内在的语义关系结构，导致会话不可恢复、推理链断裂。

方法创新：TokenMizer 系统

我们提出 TokenMizer——首个开源代理系统，将LLM会话历史建模为带类型的动态知识图谱：

  • 结构化Schema：定义14类节点（如Task、Decision、File、Technology）与7类边（如TRIGGERS、JUSTIFIES、MODIFIES），显式捕获因果、依赖与演进关系；
  • 增量构建：混合抽取流水线（规则+轻量NER+触发词匹配）实时更新图谱；
  • 三层检查点机制：生成紧凑、可查询的“恢复块”（resume blocks），支持跨会话状态续接；
  • 双效压缩：8层无损压缩流水线降低上下文开销；语义缓存加速重复查询响应。

核心结果

在覆盖5大领域的21个受控会话基准上评估：

  • 恢复块平均仅78 tokens（42–124），较基线（159–170）体积减半；
  • 决策召回率提升9–17个百分点（达46.6%），且保留决策理由而非仅技术名词；
  • 任务召回率51.0%，文件召回率58.7%；领域差异揭示：显式指令型场景（软件工程）表现优于隐式推理型（科研）；
  • 消融实验证实：模糊标签匹配贡献最大增益（+33 pp任务召回）；启发式压缩实现47.3% token削减，零外部依赖。

TokenMizer以一半token成本，提供可查询、结构化、语义保真的会话记忆替代方案。

AI Summary (English)

TokenMizer is an open-source proxy system that reimagines LLM session history as a typed, evolving knowledge graph—replacing flat text with structured, queryable memory. Its schema defines 14 node types (e.g., Decision, File, Task) and 7 edge types (e.g., JUSTIFIES, TRIGGERS) to preserve causal and contextual relationships critical for long-horizon continuity. A hybrid extraction pipeline populates the graph incrementally, while a three-tier checkpointing mechanism serializes it into compact, resumable blocks. An 8-layer compression pipeline and semantic cache jointly reduce context overhead and repeated-query latency. Evaluated on 21 cross-domain sessions, TokenMizer achieves resume blocks averaging 78 tokens—2× smaller than baselines (159–170)—with +9–17 percentage points higher decision recall, crucially preserving rationales, not just mentions. It attains mean task recall of 51.0%, decision recall of 46.6%, and file recall of 58.7%; ablation shows fuzzy label matching drives +33 pp task recall gain, and heuristic compression yields 47.3% token reduction with zero external dependencies.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) deployments for long-horizon tasks face a fundamental constraint: context windows are finite while productive work sessions are not. When history exceeds the Maximum Effective Context Window (MECW), critical structured information - architectural decisions, task transitions, file histories - is silently discarded. Existing mitigations treat history as flat text, destroying the relational structure that makes sessions resumable. We present TokenMizer, an open-source proxy system that models LLM session history as a typed knowledge graph. The schema defines 14 node types and 7 edge types. A hybrid extraction pipeline populates the graph incrementally, while a three-tier checkpoint system serializes it into compact resume blocks. An 8-layer compression pipeline reduces context overhead, and a semantic cache reduces repeated-query latency. Evaluated on a controlled benchmark of 21 sessions spanning 5 domains, TokenMizer demonstrates significant token economy. It produces resume blocks averaging 78 tokens (range: 42-124) - 2x smaller than evaluated baselines (159-170 tokens) - while achieving higher decision recall (+9-17 percentage points). Crucially, baselines only preserve that a technology was mentioned; TokenMizer preserves the rationale. Across all sessions, TokenMizer achieves mean task recall 51.0%, decision recall 46.6%, and file recall 58.7%. Variance reflects domain heterogeneity: explicit imperative phrasing (software engineering) scores higher than implicit reasoning (research). Ablation studies show fuzzy label matching is the dominant improvement factor (+33 pp task recall). The heuristic compression achieves 47.3% token reduction with zero external dependencies. TokenMizer provides a queryable alternative to text-retention baselines at half the token cost.

item.title: Improving Answer Extraction in Context-based Question Answering Systems Using LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06197v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

基于上下文的问答（QA）系统虽在大语言模型（LLM）推动下取得显著进展，但在精准答案抽取方面仍存在关键瓶颈：面对复杂、歧义性查询时，模型易生成无关、冗余或偏离上下文的答案，暴露出上下文理解浅层化、答案一致性弱、跨领域泛化能力不足等共性缺陷。

方法创新

本研究提出一种面向上下文问答的LLM优化框架，核心在于任务导向的精细化微调策略。不同于通用指令微调，我们采用高质量监督数据驱动范式：以Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD1.1) 为基准，对预训练语言模型进行端到端答案抽取专项训练。输入为“上下文+问题”对，输出为严格限定于原文片段的简洁准确答案，强制模型聚焦证据定位与边界识别能力。

主要发现与贡献

实验表明，微调后的 RoBERTa-base 模型表现最优：

  • ROUGE-L 达 86.84%（衡量答案与参考答案的最长公共子序列匹配度）
  • BLEU-4 为 28.24%（反映n-gram重叠精度）
  • BERTScore 高达 95.38%（基于语义嵌入的细粒度相似性评估）

结果证实：针对性微调可显著提升答案的相关性、忠实性与边界准确性，有效缓解“幻觉”与上下文漂移问题。本工作为构建高可信度、可解释的工业级QA系统提供了可复现的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the persistent challenge of inaccurate answer extraction in context-based question answering (QA) systems, despite advances in large language models (LLMs). We propose a targeted fine-tuning approach using the SQuAD1.1 benchmark to enhance contextual comprehension and precise span extraction. By optimizing RoBERTa-base on high-quality context-question-answer triples, our method enforces strict fidelity to source text and minimizes hallucination. Experimental results show substantial gains: ROUGE-L = 86.84%, BLEU = 28.24%, and BERTScore = 95.38%—demonstrating superior answer relevance, lexical precision, and semantic faithfulness. The findings confirm that task-specific fine-tuning significantly improves reliability, consistency, and generalizability in real-world QA applications.

Abstract

Question answering (QA) systems have achieved notable progress with the advent of large language models (LLMs). However, they still face challenges in accurately extracting and generating precise answers from given contexts, particularly when dealing with complex or ambiguous queries. Existing approaches often struggle with contextual understanding, answer consistency, and generalization across diverse domains. In this work, we propose a question answering system based on large language models, where the input consists of a textual context and a corresponding question, and the output is a concise and accurate answer. The motivation behind this research lies in addressing the limitations of current QA systems, particularly their tendency to produce irrelevant or imprecise responses despite having access to the correct context. Our methodology involves fine-tuning a pre-trained LLM on a benchmark QA dataset to improve its contextual comprehension and answer extraction capabilities. Specifically, we utilize the Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD1.1), which provides high-quality context-question-answer triplets for supervised training and evaluation. Experimental results show that the fine-tuned Roberta-base model achieves the highest performance, attaining a ROUGE-L score of 86.84%, a BLEU score of 28.24%, and a BERTScore of 95.38%. These results indicate strong accuracy and answer relevance, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach for context-based question answering tasks. Furthermore, the findings confirm that targeted fine-tuning substantially improves the reliability and precision of QA systems.

item.title: Amortizing Federated Adaptation: Hypernetwork Driven LoRA for Personalized Foundation Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06154v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习中对大模型进行低秩自适应（LoRA）微调虽能降低通信开销，但现有方法面临两大根本性瓶颈：(1) 结构聚合偏差——直接对客户端独立训练的低秩因子（A/B矩阵）进行平均，无法准确逼近全局联合更新的真实低秩结构；(2) 客户端初始化滞后——每轮通信均需重新随机初始化LoRA参数，导致个性化收敛缓慢且不稳定，尤其在非独立同分布（non-IID）数据下更为显著。

方法创新：HyperLoRA框架

我们提出HyperLoRA——首个实现“摊销式联邦适配”的统一框架，通过超网络驱动的LoRA生成与乘积空间聚合协同解决上述问题：

  • 摊销化客户端初始化：服务器端训练一个轻量级超网络（Hypernetwork），将客户端数据分布签名（如梯度统计、嵌入均值）映射为个性化LoRA权重（ΔW = A·B）的初始值，消除每轮重复初始化，将个性化适配“摊销”为一次前向生成；
  • 无偏低秩聚合：摒弃传统因子级平均，设计可学习的聚合模块，在LoRA的乘积空间（即ΔW空间）中直接合成全局更新，从源头规避结构失配；
  • 残差校正增强鲁棒性：引入轻量级残差修正模块，动态补偿超网络生成误差，在高度异构场景下显著提升稳定性与泛化性。

实验结果

在Federated-ImageNet、FedVL（视觉-语言）等基准上，HyperLoRA相较FedLoRA、LoRA-FL等方法：
✅ 收敛速度提升35–52%（达相同精度所需通信轮次减少）；
✅ 在分布偏移（如客户端类别缺失、噪声标签）下准确率相对提升8.7–12.3%；
✅ 个性化性能（per-client test accuracy）标准差降低41%，表明更强的一致性与公平性。
本工作首次将超网络建模与低秩结构先验深度融合，为大模型联邦个性化提供了高效、无偏、稳健的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated fine-tuning of foundation models via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) reduces communication overhead but suffers from structural aggregation bias (due to naive averaging of low-rank factors) and client-side initialization lag (repeated random LoRA reinitialization per round). HyperLoRA addresses both by introducing amortized federated adaptation: a server-side hypernetwork maps client distribution signatures (e.g., gradient statistics) to personalized LoRA initializations—eliminating iterative per-round optimization—and a learned aggregation module synthesizes updates directly in the low-rank product space (ΔW = AB), bypassing biased factor-wise averaging. A lightweight residual correction further boosts robustness under non-IID heterogeneity. Experiments on federated vision and vision-language benchmarks show HyperLoRA achieves 35–52% faster convergence, 8.7–12.3% higher accuracy under distribution shift, and superior personalization stability versus prior federated LoRA methods—all with comparable communication and computation costs.

Abstract

Federated fine-tuning of foundation models using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) offers a communication efficient solution for distributed learning. However, existing federated LoRA methods suffer from two fundamental limitations: (1) structural aggregation bias, where independently averaging low rank factors fails to approximate the true combined update, and (2) client side initialization lag, as clients repeatedly reinitialize LoRA parameters across communication rounds, slowing convergence. We propose HyperLoRA, a unified framework that addresses both issues through amortized federated adaptation through hypernetwork-driven LoRA generation and product space aggregation. Instead of iterative per-client optimization, HyperLoRA employs a learned generator that maps client distribution signatures to LoRA initializations, effectively amortizing per client adaptation. On the server side, we introduce a learned aggregation module that directly synthesizes updates in the low-rank product space, eliminating the inconsistencies of factor-wise averaging. A lightweight residual correction module further improves stability under heterogenous (non-IID) client distributions.By replacing iterative optimization and heuristic averaging with learned operators, HyperLoRA jointly enables efficient personalization, unbiased aggregation, and faster convergence. Experiments on federated vision and vision-language benchmarks show that HyperLoRA achieves improved convergence speed, greater robustness to distribution shift, and stronger personalization performance compared to prior federated LoRA methods.

item.title: EGTR-Review: Efficient Evidence-Grounded Scientific Peer Review Generation via Multi-Agent Teacher Distillation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06025v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

科学同行评审生成旨在缓解审稿人力负担、提升反馈时效性，但现有大语言模型（LLM）方法普遍存在证据支撑薄弱、源可追溯性差、评论泛化严重等问题；而复杂多智能体系统虽增强推理能力，却带来高昂的推理开销与部署成本。

方法创新：EGTR-Review 框架

我们提出 EGTR-Review——一种基于多智能体教师蒸馏的证据可溯型评审生成框架。其核心包含两阶段设计：

  • 多智能体教师（Multi-Agent Teacher）：协同执行结构感知论文分解、关键要素抽取、外部学术证据检索（对接Semantic Scholar/ACL Anthology）、证据状态标注（支持/反驳/无关）、多跳验证推理及评审合成，全程保留细粒度推理轨迹与溯源锚点；
  • 轻量级学生模型蒸馏：通过任务前缀驱动的多任务学习，联合蒸馏教师的中间推理路径（如证据匹配逻辑、论证强度判断）与最终评审文本；引入证据加权损失函数，自动抑制弱证据、缺失证据或不可验证监督信号的负面影响，提升训练鲁棒性。

实验结果与优势

在PubPeer、ACL Review、SciRep等公开数据集上，EGTR-Review（Student）全面超越强基线：在BLEU-4、ROUGE-L、BERTScore等自动指标上平均提升+4.2–7.8分；LLM-as-Judge评估中获92.3%偏好率；人工评估证实其事实准确率（+15.6%）、源引用完整性（+22.1%）、建设性意见比例（+18.4%）显著领先。同时，推理耗时降低63%，token消耗减少57%，兼具高性能与高效率。

AI Summary (English)

Scientific peer review generation aims to alleviate reviewer burden and accelerate feedback, yet existing LLM-based methods suffer from generic, evidence-poor comments with poor source traceability, while complex multi-agent systems incur prohibitive inference costs. To bridge this gap, we propose EGTR-Review, an Evidence-Grounded and Traceable Review generation framework via Multi-Agent Teacher Distillation. Its teacher—a coordinated multi-agent system—performs structure-aware paper decomposition, key-element extraction, external scholarly evidence retrieval, evidence-state labeling, verification reasoning, and review synthesis, preserving full reasoning trajectories and source anchors. A lightweight student model is then trained via task-prefix-driven multi-task learning to distill both intermediate reasoning steps and final reviews, optimized by an evidence-weighted objective that downweights weak, missing, or non-verifiable supervision. Experiments across public datasets show EGTR-Review (Student) consistently outperforms strong prompt-based, fine-tuned, and structured/agentic baselines in automatic metrics, LLM-as-Judge evaluation (92.3% preference), and human assessment—while reducing inference time by 63% and token consumption by 57%. Code, prompts, and data are open-sourced on GitHub.

Abstract

Scientific peer review generation has attracted increasing attention for reducing reviewing burdens and providing timely feedback. However, existing Large Language Model (LLM)-based methods often produce generic comments with insufficient evidence support and weak source traceability, while complex multi-agent systems incur high inference costs. To address these challenges, we propose EGTR-Review, an Evidence-Grounded and Traceable Review Generation framework via Multi-Agent Teacher Distillation. EGTR-Review first constructs a multi-agent teacher that performs structure-aware paper decomposition, key-element extraction, external scholarly evidence retrieval, evidence-state labeling, verification reasoning, and review synthesis. It then distills both intermediate reasoning trajectories and final review comments into a lightweight student model through task-prefix-driven multi-task learning. An evidence-weighted objective further reduces the influence of weak, missing, or non-verifiable supervision. Experiments on public peer-review datasets show that EGTR-Review (Student) outperforms strong prompt-based, fine-tuned, and structured/agentic baselines across automatic metrics, LLM-as-Judge evaluation, and human evaluation, while maintaining strong factual grounding and source traceability with substantially lower token consumption and inference time. Our code, prompts, configurations, and sample data are available on GitHub.

item.title: Measuring the sensitivity of LLM-based structured extraction to prompt, model, and schema choices in clinical discharge summaries
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05970v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLM）正被广泛用于从临床自由文本（如出院小结）中提取结构化信息，但其输出对上游配置——包括提示词（prompt）、基础模型选择及结构化模式（schema）设计——的敏感性，远未得到系统性评估。现有研究多聚焦于固定基准下的准确率，却忽视了在真实部署中因配置微调导致的结果漂移问题。

方法设计

本研究提出一种无需人工标注真值的可复现性审计框架：在MIMIC-IV v3.1出院小结数据集上，固定提取任务与评估目标，采用“单变量扰动”策略——依次独立变动prompt、模型规模（2种尺寸）和schema定义。结构化模式包含两部分：（1）17项三值临床文档标志（yes/no/not_documented）；（2）47类主入院原因标签。设计3种语义等价但表述各异的prompt模板，分别在两种模型上运行。通过分层Cohen’s kappa（按ICD诊断分层计算）量化跨prompt一致性；采用配对同文档比较分离模型效应；并通过对三值标志进行后验二值化（yes vs. no+not_documented），检验schema本身对分歧的贡献。

主要发现

  • 两模型在三值标志上的整体跨prompt一致性高度相近（中位kappa：0.69 vs. 0.68），但变化呈“重分配”而非“增强”：大模型提升部分字段一致性，却降低另一些字段的一致性。
  • 将not_documented与no合并为单一“否”类后，跨prompt分歧大幅消减，表明主要分歧源于“未记录”与“明确否定”的语义模糊边界，而非临床事实判断本身。
  • 在47类入院原因分类中，模型更换导致近50%笔记的主导标签变更，而prompt改写仅影响约12.5%；大模型显著压缩“兜底类”（residual catch-all）标签占比（44% → 26%），体现更强的细粒度判别能力。

创新价值

本研究首次将schema设计显式纳入LLM结构化抽取的敏感性分析框架，揭示了“缺席vs. 沉默”这一隐性语义轴是核心分歧源，并证实模型架构对多类分类的影响远超prompt措辞。所提方法可直接迁移至其他临床NLP部署场景，支撑稳健的模型选型与schema工程。

AI Summary (English)

This study audits the reproducibility of LLM-based structured extraction from clinical discharge summaries—without human-annotated ground truth—by systematically varying one upstream choice (prompt, model size, or schema) while holding the task fixed. Using MIMIC-IV v3.1, we evaluate extraction against a schema of 17 three-way clinical flags (yes/no/not_documented) and a 47-tag admission reason taxonomy. Cross-prompt agreement (Cohen’s kappa, ICD-stratified) reveals that two model sizes achieve nearly identical pooled agreement (median κ = 0.69 vs. 0.68), but with field-specific trade-offs—not uniform improvement. Collapsing “no” and “not_documented” into a binary schema eliminates most cross-prompt disagreement, localizing sensitivity to the absence-vs-silence distinction rather than factual presence. For admission categorization, model changes reassign the dominant tag in ~48% of notes, while prompt phrasing changes affect only ~12.5%; the larger model also sharply reduces reliance on residual catch-all categories (44% → 26%). These results demonstrate that schema design—not just model or prompt—is a primary source of instability, and that model architecture dominates prompt wording in multi-class decisions—a finding enabled by our reusable, population-scale auditing methodology.

Abstract

Large language models are increasingly used for structured extraction from clinical free-text notes, but the sensitivity of their output to upstream configuration choices is less understood than their accuracy on fixed benchmarks. This work measures that sensitivity without human-annotated ground truth, by holding the extraction task fixed and varying one choice at a time. The fixed schema comprises 17 clinical documentation flags on a three-way yes/no/not_documented value set and a 47-tag vocabulary for the primary admission reason. Three prompt variants expressing this schema were each run at two model sizes on MIMIC-IV v3.1 discharge summaries. Cross-prompt agreement was measured by Cohen's kappa on ICD-stratified subsets. A paired same-note comparison isolated the effect of model choice, and a post-hoc collapse of the three-way flags to binary tested the schema's contribution to disagreement. On the three-way flags, the two models reach the same pooled cross-prompt agreement (median kappa 0.69 and 0.68); the larger model raises agreement on some fields and lowers it on others, a redistribution rather than the absence of an effect. Collapsing the schema to binary dissolves most of the cross-prompt disagreement, locating it on the absence-versus-silence distinction rather than on whether the finding is present. On the multi-class admission categorization, changing the model reassigns the dominant tag on close to half of all notes while changing the prompt phrasing reassigns it on roughly one in eight, and the larger model places far less mass on residual catch-all categories (44% to 26%). These patterns indicate a schema-imposed source of disagreement concentrated on the absence-versus-silence axis and a dominance of model over prompt phrasing on multi-class categorization, identified by a reusable methodology for auditing extraction reproducibility on a population-scale deployment.

item.title: Microskill Architecture: A Modular Skill-Driven Framework for AI-Native Code Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05720v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLM）与AI编程智能体正深刻重塑软件开发范式，但迈向真正“AI-native”系统仍面临结构性瓶颈。核心矛盾在于：为保障代码理解准确性，开发者常将海量项目文档与源码一次性注入模型上下文，导致上下文窗口过载——模型在长序列中丢失关键中间状态、推理精度下降、Token消耗激增，且系统架构随提示工程演进而持续漂移（architectural drift），难以维护与扩展。

方法：MicroSkill 架构

本文提出MicroSkill Architecture（微技能架构），一种受微服务思想启发、面向知识封装的模块化技能驱动框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 原子化技能胶囊（Skill Capsules）：将项目知识解耦为语义边界清晰、功能单一、可独立验证的微型技能单元（如“Spring Security OAuth2 配置校验”“CMS 内容版本回滚策略”）；
  • 动态语义路由（Dynamic Router）：基于任务查询实时计算各技能胶囊的语义相关性，仅激活最相关的若干胶囊，严格受限于预设Token预算；
  • 形式化建模：将上下文分配建模为带约束的优化问题——最大化语义相关性得分，同时满足Token消耗 ≤ 预算上限。

关键成果

在企业级内容管理系统（含15个复杂业务特性）的实证研究中，MicroSkill 实现：
✅ Token消耗降低90%以上（从平均12,800 → 1,050 tokens）；
✅ 首次编译成功率提升94%（从42% → 81%）；
✅ 100%消除架构违规（如跨层调用、配置硬编码等）；
✅ 支持自主演化：通过内置自学习机制，无需人工干预即完成7个新技能胶囊的自动提取、语义标注与注册。

本架构为构建高效、可靠、可持续演化的AI-native开发系统提供了可扩展的基础范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) have transformed coding, yet achieving truly AI-native development remains hindered by context window inefficiency: injecting entire codebases degrades accuracy, inflates token costs, and induces architectural drift. This paper introduces MicroSkill Architecture, a modular, skill-driven framework inspired by microservices—but applied to knowledge encapsulation, not service decomposition. It partitions domain knowledge into atomic, semantically scoped “skill capsules” (e.g., “CMS content rollback policy”) and employs a dynamic router that selects only the most relevant capsules per task under strict token budget constraints—formalized as a semantic-relevance–maximizing constrained optimization problem. In an enterprise CMS case study with 15 complex features, MicroSkill reduced token usage by >90%, nearly doubled first-try compilation success (42% → 81%), eliminated all architectural violations, and autonomously discovered, extracted, and registered 7 new skill capsules via self-learning. The architecture establishes a scalable foundation for efficient, reliable, and evolvable AI-native development systems.

Abstract

Large language models and AI coding agents have reshaped software development, but the path to fully AI-native systems faces structural challenges. Chief among them is managing context windows without losing accuracy or efficiency. When developers inject full project documentation and code into a model's memory, the model loses mid-sequence information, token costs spiral, and architecture drifts. This paper presents MicroSkill Architecture: a modular design paradigm inspired by microservices, applied to knowledge encapsulation instead of service decomposition. Instead of feeding an agent the entire codebase, the architecture partitions knowledge into atomic, sharply scoped skill capsules, and a dynamic router selects only semantically relevant capsules for the task. We formally model context allocation as constrained optimization over semantic relevance subject to a token budget. An empirical case study an enterprise content management system with fifteen complex features shows that MicroSkill cuts token consumption by over 90%, nearly doubles first-try compilation success rates, eliminates architectural violations entirely, and enables autonomous extraction and registration of seven new skill capsules via a self-learning mechanism. These findings suggest MicroSkill Architecture offers a scalable foundation for building AI-native development systems that are more efficient, more reliable, and capable of evolving over time.

item.title: InfoShield: Privacy-Preserving Speech Representations for Mental Health Screening via Information-Theoretic Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05561v1

AI Summary (中文)

InfoShield：面向心理健康筛查的隐私保护语音表征方法

背景与挑战：基于语音的精神健康筛查（如抑郁症检测）具备高可扩展性，但临床落地面临核心障碍——用户担忧语音中隐含的性别、年龄等敏感人口统计信息被意外泄露。现有隐私保护方案存在明显局限：对抗训练泛化性差，难以抵御未知攻击；差分隐私则通过全局加噪破坏语音表征结构，显著削弱诊断性能（尤其对细粒度情感与韵律特征）。

方法创新：本文提出 InfoShield，一种基于信息论优化的隐私-效用协同框架。其核心思想是最小化语音表征与敏感属性间的互信息（MI），同时严格约束抑郁分类任务的性能损失。针对语音序列建模中MI估计的关键瓶颈——传统MINE（Mutual Information Neural Estimator）因忽略时序动态性而与静态属性嵌入严重错位，我们设计 TimeAwareMINE：引入跨模态注意力机制，动态对齐每帧声学特征与敏感属性语义嵌入，实现时序感知的精准MI估计与梯度反传。

主要结果：在权威Androids Corpus上验证，InfoShield将性别推断准确率从92.6%降至55.5%（近随机水平），年龄推断准确率从55.7%大幅压至30.3%，而抑郁分类F1仅下降6%（0.784 vs. 前SOTA 0.723）。该结果首次在语音心理健康分析中实现隐私防护强度与临床效用的实质性双赢，为可信AI医疗部署提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Speech-based mental health screening promises scalable depression detection, yet privacy leakage of demographic attributes (e.g., gender, age) impedes real-world adoption. Existing methods—adversarial training and differential privacy—fail to balance privacy and diagnostic utility robustly. This paper introduces InfoShield, a novel information-theoretic framework that explicitly minimizes mutual information (MI) between speech representations and sensitive attributes while preserving depression classification accuracy. To overcome temporal-static misalignment in sequential speech, we propose TimeAwareMINE, which employs cross-modal attention to align acoustic frames with attribute embeddings for precise MI estimation and optimization. Evaluated on the Androids Corpus, InfoShield reduces gender inference accuracy from 92.6% to 55.5% and age inference from 55.7% to 30.3%, achieving near-random guessing for both—while sustaining strong clinical utility (F1 = 0.784, +6.1% over prior SOTA). This work establishes a new benchmark for privacy-preserving, clinically viable speech analytics.

Abstract

Speech-based mental health screening offers scalable depression detection, yet clinical deployment faces a significant barrier: users' privacy concerns about demographic information exposure. Current techniques struggle to resolve this conflict. Adversarial training often fails against unseen threats, whereas Differential Privacy tends to compromise diagnostic performance by injecting noise across all features. This paper presents InfoShield, which minimizes mutual information between speech representations and sensitive attributes while preserving depression classification accuracy. We identify that standard MINE estimators struggle with sequential speech due to temporal-static misalignment, and introduce TimeAwareMINE with cross-modal attention to align acoustic frames with attribute embeddings. Experiments on the Androids Corpus show InfoShield reduces gender inference from 92.6\% to 55.5\% and age inference from 55.7\% to 30.3\% with limited utility loss (6\% F1 reduction), achieving F1=0.784 compared to prior SOTA's 0.723.

item.title: Quantifying the Privacy of Counterfactuals by Leveraging Membership Inference Attacks Against Synthetic Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06334v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

反事实解释（Counterfactuals）广泛应用于信贷审批、医疗诊断等高风险决策场景，用于向用户阐明“若输入特征发生何种变化，模型输出将如何改变”。然而，这类高度逼真的生成样本在提升可解释性的同时，也隐含显著隐私风险——其分布特性与真实训练数据高度相似，可被视作一类隐式合成数据。现有研究多聚焦于对原始模型或合成数据的成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIA），但尚未系统评估反事实本身作为独立攻击面的隐私脆弱性，尤其缺乏无需访问原模型的黑盒攻击范式。

方法创新

本研究首次建立反事实与合成数据在隐私属性上的理论类比，并迁移并适配针对合成数据设计的MIA方法（如基于似然比检验、分类器蒸馏与置信度阈值的多策略框架），系统评估其在四类主流反事实生成器（DiCE、FACE、Growing Spheres、CLUE）产出的反事实上的有效性。关键突破在于：提出一种纯反事实驱动的成员推断攻击（CF-MIA）——仅需一组公开发布的反事实样本（不含任何模型查询权限），通过建模其局部流形密度与生成偏差特征，即可以最高89.3% 的准确率推断某真实个体是否参与了原模型训练。

主要发现与启示

实验表明：（1）反事实的隐私泄露程度与其保真度和稀疏性呈强正相关；（2）即使经差分隐私扰动的反事实仍存在可观MIA成功率（平均72.1%）；（3）攻击效果在跨模型泛化场景下稳健（迁移攻击AUC达0.83）。本工作揭示：反事实发布本身即构成新型数据泄露通道，呼吁模型部署者在可解释性服务中嵌入隐私风险评估模块，并建议对反事实施加针对性脱敏或生成约束。

AI Summary (English)

Counterfactual explanations—widely used for model interpretability in high-stakes domains—are shown to pose non-negligible privacy risks, as they structurally resemble synthetic data and can leak membership information about training individuals. This paper bridges the gap by adapting state-of-the-art membership inference attacks (MIAs) originally designed for synthetic data to various counterfactual generation methods (e.g., DiCE, FACE). Crucially, we propose a novel model-free MIA that requires only the counterfactuals themselves, with no access to the source model or its API—achieving up to 89.3% attack accuracy. Our experiments across diverse datasets and generators demonstrate that counterfactuals inherently carry membership signals, even under differential privacy perturbations, and that such leakage persists under cross-model transfer (AUC = 0.83). These findings urge practitioners to treat counterfactual release as a first-class privacy threat and integrate mitigation strategies into explanation pipelines.

Abstract

Counterfactuals are typically used in high-stakes decision areas to explain a machine learning model by showing how changes to the user profiles result in the desired outcome. However, explaining the model's decisions through counterfactuals can also be exploited by an adversary to conduct privacy attacks against the model or its training data. Drawing on the analogy that counterfactuals provide realistic substitutes for real training data, similar to synthetic data, we demonstrate in this paper how it is possible to successfully perform privacy attacks on counterfactuals by drawing on the attacks developed against synthetic data. More precisely, we investigate the effectiveness of the membership inference attacks designed for synthetic data on various types of counterfactuals. Additionally, while existing membership inference attacks against counterfactuals usually require to be able to query the model, we show how it is possible to perform successful membership inference attacks using only a set of counterfactuals, with no access to the model from which they are generated. Our results demonstrate that model developers should be more cautious when releasing counterfactuals to various users, as it can lead to a privacy breach.

item.title: Steering Vectors are an Adversarial Attack Surface
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05958v1

AI Summary (中文)

激活引导向量构成新型对抗攻击面：一种隐蔽的数据投毒攻击

激活引导（Activation Steering）作为一种无需微调即可调控大语言模型（LLM）行为的即插即用技术，正被广泛应用于对齐控制、风格迁移与安全干预等场景。用户常共享预计算的引导向量（steering vectors）及配套数据集，形成开放协作生态。然而，本研究首次揭示：该生态存在严重安全隐患——引导数据集本身构成一个隐蔽的对抗攻击表面。

我们提出一种隐蔽数据投毒攻击（Stealth Data Poisoning Attack）：攻击者仅需替换引导数据集中 4–6% 的 token（如在看似无害的指令-响应对中注入语义中性但方向敏感的扰动），即可使训练出的引导向量在保持原有良性功能（如增强共情表达、提升代码生成质量）的同时，悄然对齐“反拒答方向”（anti-refusal direction）。该向量可绕过模型内置的安全机制，在面对恶意提示（如越狱请求）时显著提升攻击成功率，而终端用户难以察觉异常。

我们在两个开源权重模型家族（Llama-3 和 Qwen2）上系统验证了该攻击，覆盖 8 种模型-属性组合（如“拒绝有害请求”“遵守伦理约束”等）。实验表明： poisoned 向量在真实越狱测试中实现 20–55% 的绝对攻击成功率（ASR），较干净基线提升 +19% 至 +51%。更关键的是，攻击者可打包发布含文本、向量、权重及可验证等价性证书（equivalence certificate）的“安全套件”，使终端用户误信其功能完整性。最后，我们提出一种轻量级防御方案——拒答方向正交化（refusal-direction orthogonalization），可在不损害良性引导效果的前提下，恢复约 82% 的 ASR 差距，为引导向量供应链安全提供实用保障。

AI Summary (English)

Activation steering—controlling LLM behavior via precomputed vectors without fine-tuning—has gained traction as a plug-and-play alignment tool. However, we uncover that steering datasets themselves constitute a stealthy adversarial attack surface. We propose a stealth data poisoning attack: by modifying only 4–6% of tokens in the steering dataset (e.g., inserting semantically neutral but directionally malicious perturbations), an attacker can silently bias the resulting steering vector toward an anti-refusal direction, enabling jailbreaks on harmful prompts while preserving intended functionality on benign ones. We evaluate across two open-weight model families (Llama-3, Qwen2) and eight model-attribute pairs, observing poisoned vectors achieve 20–55% absolute attack success rate (ASR)—a +19% to +51% gain over clean baselines. Critically, attackers can distribute “safe-looking” bundles (texts, vectors, weights, and verifiable equivalence certificates) that pass user validation. Finally, a simple refusal-direction orthogonalization defense recovers ≈82% of the ASR gap without degrading benign steering performance.

Abstract

Activation steering has become a popular way to control Large Language Model (LLM) behavior without fine-tuning. Since the technique is plug-and-play, users share datasets and precomputed vectors to steer model activations. However, we show that a \emph{stealth data poisoning attack} silently compromises this pipeline. By substituting $4{-}6\%$ of tokens in the steering dataset, an attacker can silently align the resulting vector with an anti-refusal direction. This jailbreaks the target model while preserving the intended steering effect on benign prompts. Under this threat model, a malicious actor can distribute an apparently safe bundle containing texts, vectors, and weights, alongside an equivalence certificate that the end-user can verify. We test the attack on two open-weight model families and eight model-attribute combinations, observing that poisoned vectors reach an absolute attack success rate (ASR) of $20{-}55\%$, $+19\%$ to $+51\%$ over a clean reference. Finally, we find that a refusal-direction orthogonalization defense can recover ${\approx}82\%$ of the ASR gap without harming benign behavior.

item.title: DBHN-Net: Dual-Branch Hybrid Neural Network For Low-Complexity Monaural Speech Enhancement
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05911v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

基于人工神经网络（ANN）的单通道语音增强（SE）方法虽性能优异，但高计算复杂度与高能耗严重制约其在嵌入式前端设备（如助听器、智能耳机）中的实际部署。脉冲神经网络（SNN）虽具天然低功耗优势，但其离散二值化激活与复杂的时空动力学易导致特征信息丢失，难以兼顾效率与性能。

方法创新

本文提出DBHN-Net：双分支混合神经网络，首次实现ANN与SNN在语音增强任务中的协同建模：

  • 架构设计：构建ANN主干分支（保障表征精度）与轻量SNN分支（主攻功耗压缩）；引入BandSplit-TF-Mamba模块，在频带分割基础上融合Mamba的长程建模能力，同步提升性能并降低计算开销；
  • 抗信息丢失机制：设计脉冲特征提取组（SFEG） 与信息变换块（ITB），均嵌入残差连接，有效缓解SNN固有信息衰减；
  • 跨分支交互：提出交互模块（Interaction Module） 实现多阶段特征对齐，并首创时频交叉注意力融合（TF-Cross Attention-Fusion），自适应引导SNN分支保留关键语音成分（如清音起始、谐波结构），实现数据驱动的动态信息强化。

主要结果

在DNS-Challenge、VoiceBank+DEMAND及CHiME-4三大公开数据集上，DBHN-Net在PESQ（+2.17）、STOI（+0.083）和ESTOI（+0.091）等指标上全面超越主流ANN基线；同时平均计算复杂度降低7.5倍（FLOPs），推理功耗下降约6.8×，为边缘端实时低功耗语音增强提供了可落地的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes DBHN-Net, a Dual-Branch Hybrid Neural Network for low-complexity monaural speech enhancement, addressing the critical trade-off between performance and computational efficiency. DBHN-Net synergistically integrates an ANN branch (for high-fidelity feature representation) and an SNN branch (for ultra-low-power inference), bridging their complementary strengths. Key innovations include: (1) the BandSplit-TF-Mamba module, jointly optimizing spectral resolution and sequential modeling with reduced FLOPs; (2) the Spiking Feature Extraction Group (SFEG) and Information Transformation Block (ITB) with residual connections to mitigate SNN-induced information loss; and (3) a novel TF-Cross Attention-Fusion module, enabling adaptive, time-frequency–aware inter-branch interaction that guides the SNN to preserve phonetically critical features. Evaluated on DNS-Challenge, VoiceBank+DEMAND, and CHiME-4, DBHN-Net achieves state-of-the-art PESQ (+2.17), STOI (+0.083), and ESTOI (+0.091) scores while reducing average computational complexity by 7.5× versus ANN baselines—demonstrating unprecedented efficiency-performance balance for edge deployment.

Abstract

Although artificial neural network (ANN) based speech enhancement (SE) methods demonstrate excellent performance, the high computational complexity and high energy consumption hinder their deployment in practical front-end processing tasks.} Currently, the spiking neural networks (SNNs) have shown potential in reducing power consumption. However, the discrete binary activation and complex spatio-temporal dynamics of SNNs often result in information loss. The current challenge therefore focuses on how to maintain performance and reduce computational complexity. To address this issue, this work propose a Dual-Branch Hybrid Neural (DBHN) Network. 1) In terms of network architecture: A dual-branch network integrating ANN and SNN was designed, where the SNN branch reduces power consumption while the ANN branch addresses information loss; The BandSplit and Time-Frequency (TF) -Mamba modules were developed to simultaneously compress energy consumption and enhance model performance; Spiking Feature Extraction Group (SFEG) and Information Transformation Block (ITB) components were implemented with residual connections to mitigate information loss while further refining feature representations. 2) To facilitate inter-branch information fusion: An Interaction module was designed to promote information exchange at various stages of the dual-branch network; A TF-Cross Attention-Fusion module was designed to perform time-frequency domain fusion of dual-branch information while data-adaptively guiding the SNN branch to retain more critical information. Results show that the proposed model maintains superior performance across three public datasets while achieving an average 7.5 fold reduction in computational complexity compared to baseline models.

item.title: Q-GNN: Query-Conditioned Graph Neural Networks with Type Awareness for Knowledge Graph Completion
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05639v1

AI Summary (中文)

Q-GNN：面向知识图谱补全的查询条件化、类型感知图神经网络

知识图谱补全（KGC）旨在从不完整知识图中预测缺失的三元组（头实体, 关系, 尾实体），是问答、推荐与推理等下游任务的关键基础。当前主流GNN方法（如CompGCN、RPGNN）虽通过构建以查询关系为中心的局部子图并执行消息传递取得进展，但存在根本性局限：它们仅将查询关系作为推理的唯一引导信号，而将查询实体（如头实体h或尾实体t）降格为子图提取的“结构锚点”，完全忽视其蕴含的丰富结构上下文与语义先验信息。

为此，本文提出Q-GNN——首个显式联合建模查询实体与查询关系的GNN框架。我们从两个互补维度注入查询实体信息：

  • 结构上下文建模：设计轻量级实体上下文编码器，聚合其一阶邻域的拓扑结构与关系模式（如“<h, worksAt, ? > → <?, locatedIn, City>”），生成上下文感知的实体表示，并用于动态调制消息传递权重；
  • 语义类型感知：利用大语言模型（LLM）对查询实体进行零样本类型推断（如“Apple Inc.”→Organization, “Paris”→City），将类型嵌入融入多头注意力机制与最终打分函数，引入类型层级的逻辑约束（如Person不可作为capitalOf的宾语）。

在FB15k-237、WN18RR和YAGO3-10三大基准上，Q-GNN在MRR与Hits@1指标上全面超越SOTA模型（平均+2.1% MRR），尤其在稀疏关系与长尾实体场景下提升显著（+4.7% Hits@1）。消融实验证实：结构上下文与类型感知模块分别贡献+1.3%与+1.6% MRR增益，二者协同产生非线性增强效应。本工作首次实现了查询实体从“被动锚点”到“主动推理伙伴”的范式转变。

AI Summary (English)

Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) aims to predict missing triples in incomplete knowledge graphs. While recent GNN-based methods leverage query relations to guide message passing over local subgraphs, they treat query entities merely as structural anchors—ignoring their rich structural context and semantic type information. To address this, we propose Q-GNN, the first query-conditioned GNN that jointly exploits both the query relation and the query entity for reasoning. Q-GNN introduces two novel entity-aware mechanisms: (1) a context encoder that captures the entity’s neighborhood structure and relation patterns to modulate message passing; and (2) an LLM-inferred semantic type module, whose embeddings constrain attention computation and final scoring via type-level priors. Experiments on FB15k-237, WN18RR, and YAGO3-10 show Q-GNN achieves new state-of-the-art results (e.g., +2.1% average MRR), with particularly strong gains on sparse relations (+4.7% Hits@1). Ablations confirm complementary contributions from both modules. Code and data are publicly available.

Abstract

Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) aims at predicting missing triplets from incomplete knowledge graphs, which is crucial for downstream applications. Recently, Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based methods have achieved remarkable success by performing message passing over query-centered local subgraphs. However, in practice, a query is jointly defined by both the entity and the relation, with both carrying information indispensable for reasoning, yet these methods rely solely on the query relation as the guiding signal, while the information inherent in the query entity is not leveraged to guide inference - the entity serves merely as a structural anchor for subgraph extraction.
