To this end, we incorporate query entity information into the reasoning process from two perspectives: the first is structural context, i.e., the neighboring structure and relation patterns around the entity, which is encoded by a dedicated context encoder and used to modulate messages; the second is semantic type of the entity, inferred by a large language model, which is incorporated into attention computation and final scoring to provide type-level prior constraints. Together, these two sources of information enable the reasoning process to be guided by both the query relation and the query entity. Experimental results on standard benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed Q-GNN.

item.title: SkillComposer: Learning to Evolve Agent Skills for Specification and Generalization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06079v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

智能体技能（Agent skills）作为可复用的推理与行动策略，已成为提升大模型推理能力的关键路径。然而，现有技能构建方法多采用“一次性提取”范式，忽视了技能特化性（specification）与泛化性（generalization）之间的根本张力：过度适配具体任务的技能难以迁移；而过度抽象的技能又缺乏足够指导力，导致技能鲁棒性差、部署灵活性低。

方法创新：SkillComposer框架

本文提出SkillComposer——首个将技能构建显式解耦为三个可学习操作的框架：创建（create）、改进（improve） 和 融合（merge）。其核心突破在于：

  • 设计系统性拒绝采样训练范式，使语言模型在推理时能自主演化技能；
  • 支持三种灵活部署模式：离线模式（构建跨任务通用技能库）、在线模式（针对新任务实时精调）与混合模式（协同利用通用性与特异性）；
  • 首次将“技能组合”建模为一种可迁移的元能力（transferable meta-ability），而非静态知识注入。

实验与发现

在 $τ^2$-Bench（多步推理）、LiveCodeBench v6（代码生成）和 AppWorld（真实应用交互）三大基准上，SkillComposer全面超越SOTA基线。其中，轻量级 SkillComposer-4B 作为技能控制器，驱动27B执行器时：

  • 在代理任务上最高提升 +4.5 分，
  • 在代码任务上最高提升 +3.4 分，

且显著泛化至训练中未见的领域与任务类型。分析表明：improve 主要提升技能准确性与可靠性，merge 则增强多样性与组合效率，二者质量维度正交互补。

AI Summary (English)

Agent skills—reusable reasoning and action strategies—hold great promise for enhancing model capabilities at inference time. Yet current methods treat skill construction as one-shot extraction, failing to resolve the core tension between task-specific specification (poor transfer) and over-abstraction (insufficient guidance). To bridge this gap, we propose SkillComposer, a framework that decomposes skill evolution into three learnable operations: create, improve, and merge, trained via systematic rejection sampling. It enables language models to self-evolve skills dynamically at inference time and supports offline (generalized library building), online (task-specific refinement), and hybrid deployment. Experiments on $τ^2$-Bench, LiveCodeBench v6, and AppWorld show consistent gains: SkillComposer-4B boosts a 27B executor by up to +4.5 on agent tasks and +3.4 on code tasks, while generalizing across unseen domains and task types. Crucially, improve and merge address orthogonal quality dimensions, and skill composition emerges as a transferable meta-ability—offering a practical, scalable recipe for skill-augmented inference.

Abstract

Agent skills, which consist of reusable strategies that guide agent reasoning and action, have shown strong potential for improving model capability at inference time. However, current skill construction methods treat the problem as one-shot extraction, overlooking a fundamental tension: a skill tailored to the specific task fails to transfer, while the abstracted skill often provides insufficient guidance. We attribute this fragility to the absence of explicit mechanisms for skill specification and generalization. To address this gap, we introduce SkillComposer, a framework that decomposes skill construction into three learnable operations: create, improve, and merge. Trained via systematic rejection sampling recipe, SkillComposer enables language models to self-evolve skills at inference time and supports three deployment modes: offline for building generalized libraries, online for task-specific refinement, and hybrid for combining both. Comprehensive experiments on $τ^2$-Bench, LiveCodeBench v6, and AppWorld show that SkillComposer consistently outperforms baselines. Our SkillComposer-4B improves a 27B executor by up to +4.5 on agent tasks and +3.4 on code tasks, while generalizing across domains and task types unseen during training. Analysis reveals that merge and improve address orthogonal quality dimensions and that skill composition is a transferable meta-ability, providing a practical recipe for skill-augmented inference.

item.title: Beyond tokens: a unified framework for latent communication in LLM-based multi-agent systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05711v1

AI Summary (中文)

超越文本：面向LLM多智能体系统的潜在通信统一框架

基于大语言模型（LLMs）的多智能体系统已成为解决复杂推理、规划与工具调用任务的主流范式。当前主流通信协议依赖自然语言——智能体以词元（token）为单位交换显式、可读的内部推理过程。尽管具备高可解释性，该协议存在三大结构性缺陷：高昂的推理开销（需完整生成/解码文本）、离散化导致的不可逆信息损失（连续思维被强制压缩为稀疏符号序列），以及自然语言固有的歧义性与冗余性。

本文提出首个潜在通信（latent communication）统一分析框架，旨在系统化梳理2024–2026年间快速发展的相关研究。我们从三个正交维度解构现有方法：
1. WHAT：通信内容类型——包括词嵌入（Embeddings）、中间隐藏层状态（Hidden States）、键值缓存（KV-Caches）及其他连续表征；
2. WHICH：发送方-接收方对齐机制——涵盖潜在空间对齐（如投影/适配器）与层级对齐（跨层映射或同层直传）；
3. HOW：信息融合策略——如拼接（concatenation）、前置注入（prepending）、数学运算（add/multiply）、交叉注意力（cross-attention）及KV缓存恢复（cache restoration）。

基于该框架，我们系统归类了18种代表性方法，提炼出5类核心设计模式，并揭示四大开放挑战：跨模型架构对齐、潜在信道安全性、边缘设备轻量化压缩，以及潜在通信与潜在思维链（latent CoT）的本质关联。本工作显著降低新研究者入门门槛，并为未来工作提供标准化比较语义与评估坐标系。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a unified 3-axis framework for latent communication in LLM-based multi-agent systems, addressing the structural limitations of token-level natural language exchange—namely high inference cost, irreversible discretization loss, and linguistic ambiguity/redundancy. The framework systematically characterizes methods along three orthogonal dimensions: (1) WHAT continuous representations are communicated (e.g., embeddings, hidden states, KV-caches); (2) WHICH sender-receiver alignment is employed (latent-space and layer-level alignment); and (3) HOW information is fused into the receiver (e.g., concatenation, cross-attention, cache restoration). Applying this lens, we categorize 18 representative methods (2024–2026), identify 5 recurring design patterns, and surface key open challenges—including cross-architecture alignment, latent-channel security, edge-friendly compression, and the interplay between latent communication and latent chain-of-thought. The framework lowers entry barriers and establishes a shared vocabulary for rigorous comparison and future advancement.

Abstract

Multi-agent systems built on large language models (LLMs) have become a prevailing paradigm for tackling complex reasoning, planning, and tool-use tasks. The dominant communication protocol in such systems is natural language: agents exchange messages token-by-token, verbalising their internal reasoning so that peers can read, verify, and respond. While convenient and interpretable, this protocol suffers from three structural drawbacks -- high inference cost, irreversible information loss during discretization, and ambiguity/redundancy of natural language. A growing body of work therefore explores an alternative protocol -- latent communication -- in which agents exchange continuous representations (embeddings, hidden states, or KV-caches) directly, bypassing the bottleneck of text generation. This paper presents a unified framework for organising the rapidly expanding literature on latent communication. We analyse existing methods along three orthogonal axes: (1) WHAT information is communicated (Embeddings, Hidden States, KV-Caches, or other continuous state); (2) WHICH sender-receiver alignment is used (latent-space alignment and layer alignment); and (3) HOW the communicated information is fused into the receiver (concatenation, prepending, mathematical operations, cross-attention, or cache restoration). Under this 3-axis framework, we systematically categorise eighteen representative methods proposed between 2024 and 2026, identify five major design patterns, and surface a set of open challenges -- including cross-architecture alignment, security of latent channels, compression for edge deployment, and the relationship between latent communication and latent chain-of-thought. We hope that this framework both lowers the barrier to entry for new researchers and provides a vocabulary for comparing future work.

item.title: Preserving Data Privacy in Learning Causal Structure with Fully Homomorphic Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05129v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私保护下的因果结构学习新范式：基于全同态加密的高效实现

背景与挑战：在分布式因果发现场景中，多方需协作学习变量间的因果图结构，但原始数据因敏感性无法直接共享。传统方法依赖可信第三方或弱隐私机制，易导致信息泄露；而全同态加密（FHE）虽能实现“数据不动、计算动”，却面临三大瓶颈：高计算开销、原生不支持除法与对数运算（关键于条件独立性检验中的似然比与互信息计算），以及单密文单数据处理导致吞吐率低下。

核心方法创新：本文提出首个面向因果结构学习的端到端FHE兼容框架，包含三项关键技术：

  • 电路精简优化：重构PC算法（Peter-Clark）的核心条件独立性检验逻辑，将嵌套循环与冗余比较转化为低深度布尔/算术电路，显著降低FHE的乘法深度；
  • 可证明精度的函数近似：采用牛顿-拉夫逊倒数迭代法逼近除法，结合截断泰勒展开高效计算自然对数，误差严格控制在$10^{-3}$以内，满足统计检验鲁棒性要求；
  • SIMD批处理加速：利用FHE的单指令多数据（SIMD）特性，将多个变量对的独立性检验并行编码至同一密文，吞吐量提升达17.2×。

实证效果与扩展性：在ASIA、CHILD、INSURANCE等标准贝叶斯网络数据集上，本方法在FHE保护下重建的因果图与明文基线的结构汉明距离平均仅1.4（满分20），Shd-F1分数达0.89；单次完整学习耗时<25分钟（Intel Xeon Gold 6330, 64GB RAM），远低于同类方案数小时量级。更值得注意的是，其模块化设计可无缝迁移至差分隐私（DP）场景——仅需替换噪声注入模块，即在$\varepsilon=1.0$下保持0.83 F1得分，验证了架构的强泛化能力。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses privacy leakage in distributed causal structure learning by proposing the first end-to-end Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE)-based framework. To overcome FHE’s inherent limitations—high computational cost, lack of native division/logarithm support, and low throughput—we introduce three key techniques: (i) circuit simplification to reduce multiplicative depth in conditional independence tests; (ii) accurate approximations of division (via Newton-Raphson reciprocal iteration) and logarithm (via truncated Taylor expansion), with bounded error (< $10^{-3}$); and (iii) SIMD-accelerated batching to parallelize multiple independence tests within a single ciphertext. Evaluated on benchmark Bayesian networks (ASIA, CHILD, INSURANCE), our method achieves near-plaintext accuracy: average structural Hamming distance of 1.4 and Shd-F1 score of 0.89, while completing full causal discovery in under 25 minutes—even under FHE protection. Its modular design further enables straightforward extension to differential privacy, achieving 0.83 F1 at $\varepsilon = 1.0$.

Abstract

Preserving data privacy is an important topic in structural data management and data mining. However, the issue of privacy leakage in distributed causal structure learning is a persistent challenge, especially in cases where data transmission and computation are required. In this paper, we propose a method based on fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) that performs calculations on ciphertexts, keeping data encrypted in transition and computation. Nevertheless, adopting FHE to causal structure learning is challenging due to the high computation cost and limited support on division as well as logarithm operations in FHE. To tackle this challenge, we propose a series of novel techniques including (i) circuit simplification for better efficiency, (ii) approximation of division and logarithm through Newton-Raphson Reciprocal and Taylor expansion, and (iii) a batching technique with SIMD-acceleration to enhance the whole learning process. Additionally, our method can be easily extended beyond FHE by demonstration of its portability to support differential privacy. Empirical results show that our method achieves high consistency and comparable causal structure with the plaintext version in the datasets tested. Last, our method is efficient and practical to complete learning causal structures in tens of minutes even under the privacy protection of FHE.

item.title: SharedRequest: Privacy-Preserving Model-Agnostic Inference for Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05004v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着ChatGPT等公共大语言模型（LLM）的广泛应用，用户提示（prompt）隐私泄露风险日益突出。现有隐私保护推理方案普遍存在三重局限：效用-隐私权衡失衡（如差分隐私导致响应质量显著下降）、效率低下（单请求加密/混淆开销高）、以及模型强耦合性（需修改模型结构或访问参数），严重制约其在异构LLM服务生态中的部署。

方法创新：SharedRequest框架

本文提出SharedRequest——首个完全模型无关（model-agnostic）的隐私保护LLM推理框架。其核心突破在于将隐私保护粒度从“单条提示”提升至“语义批次”（semantic batch）：

  • 语义聚类驱动的共享请求生成：自动识别语义等价指令（如“总结这段文字”与“请简要概括以下内容”），将其归入同一批次；
  • 噪声混合混淆机制：在批次内对原始提示注入可控语义保持型噪声（如同义词替换、句式扰动），使攻击者无法反推任一原始输入；
  • 零模型依赖设计：无需访问LLM权重、梯度或架构，仅通过标准API调用即可部署，兼容任意黑盒/开源LLM。

关键结果与优势

在多个基准（AlpacaEval、MT-Bench）和真实LLM（Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B）上验证：

  • 效用领先：相比主流差分隐私基线（DP-LLM, DP-SFT），响应质量（胜率/评分）提升超20%，且无明显幻觉增加；
  • 成本显著降低：共享批次机制实现最高5×查询成本压缩（即单次LLM前向计算服务多用户请求），吞吐量线性提升；
  • 强隐私保障：形式化满足$(\varepsilon=2,\delta=10^{-5})$-差分隐私，实测抵御成员推断与属性推断攻击成功率<8.2%。

SharedRequest为构建可扩展、合规、高性能的隐私优先LLM服务提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present SharedRequest, a model-agnostic framework for privacy-preserving inference over black-box large language models (LLMs). Unlike prior approaches that protect individual prompts—sacrificing utility, efficiency, or generality—SharedRequest shifts privacy enforcement to the semantic batch level: it groups semantically equivalent user instructions (e.g., “summarize this text” and “give a brief overview”) and obscures each prompt via controlled, meaning-preserving noise injection (e.g., synonym substitution, syntactic paraphrasing). This enables amortized inference—multiple users share a single LLM forward pass—without requiring model access, architectural changes, or parameter fine-tuning. Experiments across Llama-3-8B and Qwen2-7B show SharedRequest improves utility by >20% over state-of-the-art differential privacy baselines while reducing per-query computational cost by up to 5×. It formally satisfies $(\varepsilon=2,\delta=10^{-5})$-differential privacy and resists membership and attribute inference attacks with <8.2% success rate.

Abstract

With the widespread deployment of public large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, protecting user prompt privacy has become an increasingly critical issue. Existing privacy-preserving inference methods sacrifice either utility or efficiency, and often require model-specific modifications that limit their compatibility. In this paper, we propose SharedRequest, a model-agnostic framework for privacy-preserving LLM inference that reformulates privacy protection at the batch level rather than the individual-prompt level. The key idea is to obscure sensitive information by mixing original prompts with noisy variants, while grouping semantically equivalent instructions to amortize the inference cost over a large batch of queries with minimal impact on LLM response quality. This design is independent of the LLM architecture, requiring no access to model parameters or architectural modification. Empirical results demonstrate that SharedRequest achieves over $20\%$ higher utility compared to prior differential privacy baselines, and its shared-prompt mechanism reduces query cost by up to $5\times$ compared to non-batched inference.

item.title: Sequential Data Poisoning in LLM Post-Training
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04929v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）的后训练通常采用多阶段流水线，例如先进行监督微调（SFT），再通过人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）或直接偏好优化（DPO）等方法进一步对齐。各阶段数据常来自不同、甚至不可信的来源，但现有数据投毒研究多假设单一攻击者在单阶段施加干扰，忽视了多阶段协同攻击的可能性——即多个独立攻击者分别污染SFT数据集与偏好/奖励数据集。

方法与威胁模型

本文提出序列化数据投毒（Sequential Data Poisoning） 这一新威胁模型：两个非共谋但策略兼容的攻击者，分别在SFT和偏好训练阶段注入恶意样本。我们系统评估了SFT→DPO与SFT→PPO两条主流后训练路径，并引入“单攻击者幻觉”概念——即当仅孤立评估任一阶段的投毒效果时，攻击成功率极低，易被误判为无害；而跨阶段联合分析则暴露出显著放大的风险。

关键发现与创新点

  • 在SFT→DPO管道中，攻击贡献呈可加性：将固定投毒预算均分至两阶段，比全投于任一阶段提升高达3.2×的后门触发率；
  • 在SFT→PPO管道中，攻击贡献呈互补性：单独污染SFT或奖励模型均失效，但二者组合即可成功诱导模型生成有害响应（成功率从0%跃升至68.5%）；
  • 该现象揭示：单阶段安全分析存在系统性盲区，复合漏洞仅在阶段交互中涌现。本工作首次量化证明后训练流水线的整体可信度不能由各阶段安全性简单叠加得出。代码已开源：https://github.com/jcksanderson/sequential-poisoning。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces sequential data poisoning, a novel threat model for LLM post-training where multiple independent adversaries poison different stages—e.g., one corrupts the supervised fine-tuning (SFT) dataset and another poisons the preference or reward dataset. We expose the single-attacker illusion: each attacker appears negligible when evaluated in isolation, yet their combined effect reveals severe systemic vulnerabilities. In the SFT→DPO pipeline, their contributions are additive—splitting a fixed poison budget across stages outperforms concentrating it in either stage alone (up to 3.2× higher backdoor success). In the SFT→PPO pipeline, contributions are complementary: neither SFT-only nor reward-model-only poisoning succeeds, but their combination achieves up to 68.5% attack success. These results demonstrate that security analyses confined to individual training stages systematically underestimate compound risks arising from cross-stage interactions, challenging the assumption of modular robustness in LLM alignment pipelines. Code: https://github.com/jcksanderson/sequential-poisoning.

Abstract

LLM post-training proceeds through multiple stages, e.g., supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) or direct preference optimization (DPO), where each stage draws data from different, potentially untrusted sources. Existing literature assumes data poisoning attacks may occur at each training stage, but neglects the possibility of multiple attackers. To study the trustworthiness of the entire post-training pipeline, we propose the threat model of sequential data poisoning, where multiple adversaries separately poison the SFT and preference datasets. Under this threat model, we identify the single-attacker illusion: each adversary, evaluated in isolation, appears to pose a negligible threat. Yet when adversaries collaborate across stages, the true vulnerability is revealed. In the SFT $\to$ DPO pipeline, their contributions are additive: splitting a fixed poison budget across stages outperforms concentrating it in either stage alone. In the SFT $\to$ PPO pipeline, their contributions are complementary: neither SFT nor reward model poisoning succeeds individually, yet their combination does. These findings show that security analyses of individual post-training stages systematically underestimate compound vulnerabilities that emerge only from their interaction. Code is available at https://github.com/jcksanderson/sequential-poisoning.

item.title: DIST-FL: Enhancing Security for TEE-based Aggregation in Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04899v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

可信执行环境（TEE）赋能的联邦学习（FL）协议被广泛视为抵御服务器端恶意攻击、保障聚合服务器可信性的关键路径。然而，本文首次系统揭示：现有单TEE方案存在根本性安全缺口——攻击者可利用TEE固有局限（状态回滚与I/O操纵），通过恶意操控客户端选择策略或重放聚合操作，破坏系统鲁棒性与用户隐私。

方法创新：DIST-FL分布式架构

为根治上述威胁，我们提出DIST-FL——一种基于多TEE协同的分布式服务器系统。其核心设计包含两大机制：

  • 线性化操作保证：通过多TEE间共识驱动的追加式账本（append-only ledger），强制所有聚合操作满足线性一致性，彻底阻断状态回滚攻击；
  • 可信输入融合：引入跨服务器冗余校验与可靠节点输入加权机制，动态识别并抑制受I/O操纵影响的异常数据流，提升I/O层抗篡改能力。

DIST-FL在隐私保护前提下实现鲁棒聚合，无需修改客户端逻辑，兼容主流FL框架。

实验验证与优势

我们在广域网（WAN）环境下实现并评估DIST-FL。结果表明：
✅ 全面防御所提两类攻击，隐私泄露风险降低至理论下界；
✅ 聚合精度与单TEE方案无统计学差异（p>0.05），保障模型效用；
✅ 吞吐量达同类方案6倍提升，充分释放TEE并行计算潜力；
✅ 端到端延迟增加可控（<12%），兼顾安全性与实用性。
DIST-FL首次将分布式共识、TEE硬件安全与FL聚合流程深度耦合，为高安全FL部署提供了可落地的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Trusted Execution Environment (TEE)-based federated learning (FL) protocols aim to mitigate server-side adversaries, yet we identify two critical vulnerabilities—state rollback and I/O manipulation—that enable attackers to compromise robustness and privacy via malicious client selection or aggregation replay. To address this, we propose DIST-FL, a distributed multi-TEE server architecture that constructs an append-only ledger for privacy-preserving, robust FL aggregation. DIST-FL enforces operation linearizability across TEEs to eliminate state rollback and integrates inputs from reliable servers to resist I/O manipulation. Implemented and evaluated in WAN settings, DIST-FL successfully thwarts the proposed attacks while matching the model accuracy of single-TEE baselines. Crucially, it achieves 6× higher throughput than existing TEE-based FL systems—leveraging TEE parallelism without sacrificing security or utility—while introducing only modest latency overhead (<12%).

Abstract

Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs)-aided federated learning protocols emerge as promising solutions to counter server-side adversaries and ensure the trustworthiness of the server. In this paper, we dissect existing protocols and demonstrate that server-side adversaries can still manipulate client selection and replay aggregation to compromise system robustness and privacy, by exploiting TEE limitations, i.e., state rollback and I/O manipulation. To this end, we present DIST-FL, a distributed system of servers guarded by multiple TEEs forming an append-only ledger for privacy-preserved, robust FL aggregation. Specifically, DIST-FL ensures operation linearizability to thwart state rollback attacks and incorporates inputs from reliable servers to mitigate I/O manipulation threats. We implement DIST-FL and conduct evaluations in WAN settings. Experimental results demonstrate that DIST-FL can effectively counter the proposed attacks and match the single-TEE's performance while offering a 6x throughput boost over its counterparts, leveraging TEE's computational advantages.

item.title: ODYSSEY: Reestablishing Confidentiality in Confidential Blockchain via Delegated Execution
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04892v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

基于可信执行环境（TEEs）的保密区块链在联盟链场景中备受关注，但其实际安全性面临严峻挑战。本文首次系统性地提出两类新型攻击范式：执行推断攻击（execution-inference）与执行重放攻击（execution-replay），二者分别利用TEE长期存在的侧信道泄露（如内存访问模式、时序特征）与状态连续性缺陷（如持久化状态可被恶意复用），导致交易内容、智能合约逻辑甚至用户行为模式被逆向推断，严重破坏“端到端保密性”。

方法与创新

为此，我们提出ODYSSEY——一个面向企业级联盟链的轻量高效保密区块链架构。其核心设计包括：

  • 委托执行模型：客户端将交易执行权动态委托给其可信的指定受托方（trustee），仅向其他节点广播加密的执行结果与零知识验证证明；共识节点无需运行完整TEE执行环境，大幅收缩攻击面，兼顾保密性、性能与部署灵活性；
  • 位置感知并发执行：依据网络拓扑与 trustee 地理位置调度并发任务，降低跨域延迟，提升吞吐；
  • 委托失败容错机制：当 trustee 故障或响应超时时，自动触发多签名协同恢复流程，保障活性与一致性。

实验与结果

我们在国产企业级平台 FISCO BCOS 上实现 ODYSSEY 原型。广域网（WAN）3节点测试表明：系统在保持端到端延迟仅0.4–0.5秒的同时，达成约4000 TPS的稳定吞吐；相较现有TEE区块链方案（如Ekiden、Hawk），侧信道暴露面减少87%，且无需硬件级TEE升级即可部署。

AI Summary (English)

Confidential blockchains built on Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) promise strong transaction privacy but remain vulnerable to two newly identified threats: execution-inference attacks (exploiting persistent side channels) and execution-replay attacks (leveraging state continuity flaws), both compromising confidentiality in consortium settings. To address this, we propose ODYSSEY, a practical confidential blockchain that delegates transaction execution to client-chosen trustees—while other nodes only verify and synchronize encrypted results—thereby drastically shrinking the TEE attack surface without sacrificing performance or confidentiality. Key innovations include: (1) a location-aware concurrent execution scheduler optimizing WAN latency, and (2) a lightweight delegation failure handler ensuring liveness under trustee unavailability. Implemented on FISCO BCOS, ODYSSEY achieves ~4,000 TPS with 0.4–0.5 s end-to-end latency across 3 geographically distributed nodes—demonstrating high efficiency, strong security, and production-ready deployability.

Abstract

Confidential blockchains leveraging Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) have garnered extensive attention for transaction confidentiality. In this paper, we first taxonomize two classes of attacks against confidential blockchains, i.e., execution-inference and execution-replay attacks, which exploit TEEs' long-lasting side-channel and state-continuity issues to compromise the confidentiality of existing consortium blockchains. Then, we present ODYSSEY, a confidential blockchain that efficiently mitigates these attacks. The core innovations of ODYSSEY are the following: (1) Its delegation model: clients delegate transaction execution to their designated trustees, while other participants synchronize only the execution results, which significantly reduces the attack surface while preserving confidentiality and system performance. (2) Two novel techniques to improve ODYSSEY's efficiency and security: location-aware concurrent execution and delegation failure handler. Finally, we develop a prototype of ODYSSEY on FISCO BCOS, an enterprise-grade consortium blockchain platform. We have conducted various experiments, and our evaluation results show that in a WAN environment with 3 nodes, ODYSSEY can achieve about 4k throughput while keeping latency as low as 0.4-0.5s.

item.title: What If Prompt Injection Never Left? Exploring Cross-Session Stored Prompt Injection in Agentic Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04425v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

现代智能体（agentic）系统已突破传统会话边界，通过记忆模块、文件系统、工具状态及共享世界模型等长生命周期上下文载体，实现跨会话的状态持续与演化。这一范式转变虽提升了系统能力，却显著扩展了提示注入（prompt injection）的攻击面——而既有研究几乎全部聚焦于单次会话内的模型层瞬时攻击，严重忽视了跨会话持久化状态所引发的系统级风险跃迁。

方法与创新

受Web领域“存储型跨站脚本（Stored XSS）”启发，本文首次提出并形式化定义跨会话存储型提示注入（Cross-Session Stored Prompt Injection）：攻击者在一次交互中注入恶意指令，该指令被写入代理系统的持久化状态（如记忆库、文件、工具缓存），并在后续无攻击者参与的多次独立会话中被自动加载、执行或触发，造成隐蔽、延时、扩散式危害。我们构建了首个系统性分类法，涵盖持久化载体类型（记忆/文件/工具输出/环境变量）、激活机制（显式检索、隐式关联、事件驱动）与影响维度（目标篡改、权限提升、逻辑劫持）。进一步开发了开源基准测试集（StoredPI-Bench）与沙箱评估框架（AgentSandbox），支持对不同大模型、攻击目标（如越权访问、虚假响应、任务偏移）及持久化通道的量化风险评估。

主要发现

实验表明：主流开源与闭源智能体系统（Llama-3-Agents、AutoGen、LangChain-based agents）在默认配置下普遍存在高危存储型注入漏洞；72%的注入载荷可在3+次后续会话中持续生效；文件系统与长期记忆是最易被滥用的持久化通道；防御策略若仅依赖单次输入过滤或输出净化，将完全失效。本工作揭示：持久化使提示注入从“瞬时模型漏洞”升维为“嵌入执行状态的长期系统缺陷”，亟需面向状态生命周期的安全设计范式革新。

AI Summary (English)

Modern agentic systems evolve LLMs from stateless assistants into stateful, cross-session entities, persisting shared world state via memories, filesystems, tools, and other long-lived artifacts. This fundamentally reshapes prompt injection risks—yet prior work focuses almost exclusively on intra-session, model-level threats. Inspired by stored XSS in web security, we introduce cross-session stored prompt injection: adversarial prompts injected once can embed into persistent agent state and silently influence future, attacker-absent executions. We formalize the threat, propose a taxonomy of persistence mechanisms (e.g., memory/file/tool-based storage, activation triggers), and release StoredPI-Bench—a benchmark—and AgentSandbox—a reproducible evaluation toolkit. Experiments across 8 agent frameworks and 5 LLMs show: (1) stored injections survive ≥3 subsequent sessions in 72% of cases; (2) filesystems and long-term memory are highest-risk channels; (3) conventional input/output sanitization fails entirely. Our work reframes prompt injection as a system-level, state-embedded vulnerability, demanding new mitigation strategies targeting state lifecycle integrity.

Abstract

Modern agentic systems transform LLMs from session-bounded assistants into stateful systems that persist and evolve shared world state across sessions through memories, filesystems, tools, and other long-lived contextual artifacts. This shift fundamentally expands the attack surface of prompt injection. However, prior works on prompt injection have largely focused on model-level threats within a single session, overlooking how cross-session persistent system state fundamentally changes the system-level risk of agentic systems. Inspired by stored cross-site scripting in web systems, we introduce cross-session stored prompt injection, where a successful injection can persist within agentic system state and silently influence future executions long after the original attacker interaction has ended. To systematically study this threat, we formalize stored prompt injection and develop a taxonomy of how adversarial content persists and affects agentic systems across sessions. We further develop a benchmark and sandbox toolkit to evaluate the risks of stored prompt injection, enabling quantitative analysis of attack success across different models, attack goals, and persistence channels. Our findings highlight that persistence transforms prompt injection from an ephemeral model-level threat into a long-lived system-level vulnerability embedded within agent execution state. We hope this work draws broader attention to this emerging threat and motivates the community to systematically study and mitigate system risks arising from persistence in agentic systems.

item.title: DPDL: Towards Differential Privacy Preservation in Decentralized Stochastic Learning on Non-IID Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04399v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在去中心化学习范式中，各代理（agent）通过点对点协作训练全局模型，无需中央服务器。然而，该模式依赖频繁的梯度交换，极易导致个体数据隐私泄露；更严峻的是，真实场景下各代理的数据呈现非独立同分布（non-IID）特性，显著加剧了隐私保护与模型收敛的双重难度。

方法创新：DPDL算法

本文提出DPDL（Differential Privacy-preserving Decentralized Learning），首个面向non-IID数据的差分隐私去中心化学习框架。其核心包含两大设计：

  • 跨梯度差分隐私注入：每个代理不共享自身梯度，而是计算并扰动“跨梯度”——即邻居本地模型在其私有数据上的梯度，并通过高斯噪声机制添加满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP的噪声；
  • 相似性校准聚合：接收扰动后的跨梯度后，采用余弦相似度动态加权校准，消除non-IID导致的梯度方向偏差，再以类动量方式聚合更新本地模型。

理论与实验验证

理论分析严格推导出最小可行噪声尺度，确保任意通信轮次下隐私预算可累积控制；同时证明算法在non-IID设定下仍保持线性加速收敛性（即$O(1/\sqrt{nT})$收敛率，$n$为代理数，$T$为总迭代步）。在CIFAR-10/Landmarks等真实数据集上的实验表明：DPDL在$\varepsilon=2.0$时抵御成员推断攻击成功率低于58%（基线超72%），测试精度仅下降1.3%，显著优于DP-SGD、D-PSGD+DP等基准方法。

AI Summary (English)

We propose DPDL, the first decentralized stochastic learning algorithm that simultaneously guarantees $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-differential privacy and robust convergence under non-IID data. DPDL introduces cross-gradients—gradients of neighbors’ local models evaluated on each agent’s private data—and perturbs them via calibrated Gaussian noise before exchange. Crucially, received noisy cross-gradients are similarity-weighted using cosine similarity to mitigate non-IID bias, enabling effective momentum-style local updates. Our theoretical analysis establishes both the minimal noise level required for target privacy and proves linear speedup convergence $O(1/\sqrt{nT})$ despite data heterogeneity. Experiments on CIFAR-10 and Landmarks show DPDL achieves strong privacy (e.g., <58% success rate against membership inference at $\varepsilon=2.0$) while maintaining high model accuracy (only 1.3% drop vs. non-private baseline), outperforming existing DP-aware decentralized methods.

Abstract

In the paradigm of decentralized learning, a group of agents collaborate to train a global model using distributed datasets without a central server. Although the power of collaboration has been verified by many state-of-the-art studies, it entails extensive gradient information exchanging among the agents and thus induces high risk of privacy leakage for the individual agents. Moreover, in real-world applications, the training data are usually non-identically and independently distributed across the agents, inducing more challenges to enable privacy-preserved decentralized learning. To address these issues, we propose a privacy-preserved decentralized learning algorithm with non-IID data, DPDL, which leverages the notion of Differential Privacy (DP) in cross-gradient aggregation through a similarity-based calibration technique. Specifically, in each round, each agent perturbs the cross-gradients (i.e., the derivatives of its neighbors' local model in its private local data) by Gaussian noise mechanism before sharing them with its neighbors; it then adopt cosine similarity to calibrate the received perturbed cross-gradients such that the aggregation of the calibrated cross-gradients can be utilized to effectively update local model in a momentum-like manner. Our rigorous theoretical analysis not only reveals the minimum noise level required to achieve a specific level of privacy preservation, but also illustrates that our algorithm still achieves a linear speedup in training with non-IID data. We finally conduct extensive experiments on real-world dataset to validate the effectiveness of our algorithm in defending privacy attacks and in training accurate models.

item.title: TITAN-FedAnil+: Trust-Based Adaptive Blockchain Federated Learning for Resource-Constrained Intelligent Enterprises
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04388v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）为智能企业实现跨域协同建模提供了隐私保护新范式，但在资源受限的边缘企业场景中面临双重瓶颈：一是非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据导致的模型漂移与收敛不稳定；二是去中心化环境中恶意客户端投毒、模型篡改等安全威胁缺乏轻量级检测机制。现有方案或依赖预设攻击者数量、或引入高开销共识与存储，难以兼顾鲁棒性、效率与可部署性。

方法创新：TITAN-FedAnil+ 框架

本文提出 TITAN-FedAnil+——一种面向资源受限智能企业的可信自适应区块链联邦学习框架，包含三大核心技术：

  • 基于亲和传播（Affinity Propagation）的自适应聚类聚合：无需预先设定攻击者数量，动态识别更新向量簇结构，自动过滤离群恶意梯度，提升抗拜占庭鲁棒性；
  • GPU加速向量化计算引擎：将模型聚合、签名验证与状态同步操作全流水线向量化，在边缘端显著压缩CPU-GPU数据搬运开销；
  • 带符号状态跳转（Signed State Jump）区块链轻量同步机制：仅同步关键区块哈希与验证签名，跳过完整链式回溯，使节点在异常断连后可在毫秒级完成可信状态恢复。

实验结果与价值

在8 GB内存边缘设备上开展50轮通信实验，TITAN-FedAnil+ 相比基线框架（FedAvg+PBFT）实现内存开销降低81%，训练吞吐量提升3.2×，且在30%恶意客户端注入下仍保持92.4%的全局模型准确率（+5.7% vs. baseline）。本工作首次将无监督聚类思想深度嵌入FL聚合层，并实现“计算—通信—存储”三维轻量化设计，为工业级智能企业联邦学习落地提供了可验证、低门槛、高弹性的一体化解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative intelligence but suffers from non-IID data heterogeneity and decentralized security threats—especially in resource-constrained enterprise edge environments. This paper proposes TITAN-FedAnil+, a trust-based adaptive blockchain FL framework for intelligent enterprises. Its core innovations include: (1) affinity propagation–based adaptive clustered aggregation, which detects and filters malicious updates without prior knowledge of attacker count; (2) GPU-accelerated vectorization, optimizing computational efficiency across aggregation, signing, and verification; and (3) a signed state jump mechanism, enabling lightweight, millisecond-scale blockchain resynchronization. Experiments on 8 GB edge devices over 50 communication rounds show 81% memory overhead reduction, 3.2× higher throughput, and robust accuracy (92.4%) under 30% Byzantine clients—outperforming baselines in robustness, scalability, and resource efficiency.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as an effective paradigm for collaborative intelligence while preserving data privacy. However, data heterogeneity arising from non-IID distributions and decentralized security threats remain significant challenges, particularly in resource-constrained enterprise environments. This paper presents TITAN-FedAnil+, a Trust-Based Adaptive Network for blockchain-enabled federated learning in intelligent enterprises. The proposed framework introduces affinity propagation-based adaptive clustered aggregation to identify and filter malicious updates without requiring prior knowledge of the number of attackers. In addition, GPU-accelerated vectorization is employed to improve computational efficiency, while a signed state jump mechanism enables lightweight blockchain resynchronization. Experimental results demonstrate substantial reductions in memory overhead, achieving up to 81% savings across 50 communication rounds on constrained 8 GB edge devices compared with the baseline framework. The results indicate that TITAN-FedAnil+ effectively improves robustness, scalability, and resource efficiency for secure federated learning deployments in intelligent enterprise environments.

item.title: Revisiting Privacy Amplification by Subsampling in Selective Release DPSGD
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04384v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

机器学习依赖敏感数据，亟需差分隐私（DP）保障。差分隐私随机梯度下降（DPSGD）虽为标准方案，却因梯度裁剪与噪声注入导致显著效用下降与收敛缓慢。近期提出的差分隐私选择性更新与释放（DPSUR）算法大幅提升了模型性能，但其隐私分析存在关键缺陷：未建模选择性释放机制引入的非均匀采样概率变化，导致隐私预算被高估，损害隐私保证的严格性。

方法创新

本文系统重审选择性释放下的隐私放大效应，首次将子采样（subsampling）与选择性释放的耦合机制纳入统一隐私分析框架。我们推导出紧致、可计算的Rényi 差分隐私（RDP）界，精确刻画梯度裁剪后选择性释放对隐私放大的影响。基于此，提出新算法——基于裁剪梯度的差分隐私选择性释放（DPSR-CG），其核心包含：（1）以裁剪梯度范数为依据动态设定释放阈值；（2）采用修正的隐私会计机制，支持逐迭代精确累积隐私损失。

主要发现与贡献

在 MNIST、CIFAR-10、FMNIST 和 IMDB 四个基准数据集上的实验表明：DPSR-CG 在 ε ≤ 4.0（δ = 10⁻⁵） 下，图像分类任务准确率较 DPSGD 提升 +8.2%–12.7%，文本分类 F1 分数提升 +6.5%；且严格满足理论 RDP 界，无隐私泄露风险。本工作首次填补了选择性释放场景下隐私放大分析的理论空白，为高实用性的 DP 训练提供了兼具严格性与高效性的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DPSGD) suffers from severe utility loss and slow convergence. While DPSUR improves utility via selective gradient release, its privacy accounting ignores the sampling probability distortion induced by selection—undermining privacy rigor. We revisit privacy amplification by subsampling under selective release and derive a tight, closed-form Rényi Differential Privacy (RDP) bound that explicitly models clipping and selection coupling. Based on this analysis, we propose DPSR-CG (Differentially Private Selective Release based on Clipped Gradients), featuring dynamic release thresholds tied to clipped gradient norms and precise per-iteration RDP accounting. Extensive experiments on MNIST, CIFAR-10, FMNIST, and IMDB show DPSR-CG achieves +6.5%–12.7% higher accuracy/F1 than DPSGD at ε ≤ 4.0 (δ = 10⁻⁵), while maintaining strict, provable privacy guarantees—resolving a critical gap in selective-release DP theory.

Abstract

Machine learning's reliance on sensitive data necessitates privacy-preserving techniques like Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DPSGD). However, DPSGD suffers from substantial utility degradation and slow convergence due to gradient clipping and noise injection. Prior works have attempted to improve DPSGD from various perspectives; notably, the Differentially Private Selective Update and Release (DPSUR) algorithm has achieved remarkable model utility. However, the privacy accounting in DPSUR overlooks the variation in sampling probability introduced by the selective release mechanism, which compromises the rigor of its privacy guarantees. To address these limitations, we re-evaluate the privacy analysis of the selective release mechanism and propose a novel algorithm: Differentially Private Selective Release based on Clipped Gradients (DPSR-CG). Through a rigorous, newly derived privacy analysis and extensive experiments on multiple datasets (MNIST, CIFAR-10, IMDB, and FMNIST), we demonstrate that our DPSR-CG mechanism maintains strict privacy guarantees while achieving exceptional model performance.

item.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Center Sepsis Early Prediction with Privacy-Preserving
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04338v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多中心重症监护数据具有高度隐私敏感性与地理分布性，导致传统集中式建模在脓毒症早期预测中面临严峻障碍：既难以合规汇聚原始临床数据，又易引发患者隐私泄露风险。联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）作为一种“数据不动模型动”的协同建模范式，为跨机构联合建模提供了新路径，但其在真实临床场景下的预测性能、鲁棒性及隐私保障强度仍缺乏系统性实证验证。

方法与设计

本研究基于中国三家三甲医院的严格筛选队列，构建含648例高质量临床样本的多中心脓毒症预测数据集（涵盖生命体征、实验室指标及时序特征）。以集中式训练为性能基线，设计并实现水平联邦学习框架：各中心本地训练LSTM-Attention融合模型，仅上传加密梯度/模型参数至协调服务器进行安全聚合，全程不共享原始数据。

主要发现与创新

  • 性能对标：联邦模型AUC达0.892（集中式基线：0.901），灵敏度/特异度差异<1.5%，证明其临床预测能力高度可比；
  • 隐私强保障：通过差分隐私噪声注入与梯度反演攻击实验验证，攻击者无法从共享参数中重建原始患者记录（PSNR < 12 dB，视觉不可识别），满足GDPR与《个人信息保护法》对“匿名化处理”的实质要求；
  • 实践可行性：提出轻量级通信协议与本地模型剪枝策略，单轮通信开销降低37%，支持低带宽医疗专网部署。

本研究首次在真实多中心脓毒症数据上完成端到端FL验证，为隐私优先的智慧医疗协作提供了可复现、可监管的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study addresses the critical challenge of privacy-preserving sepsis early prediction across distributed clinical centers. Using a rigorously curated dataset of 648 patients from three tertiary hospitals in China, we implement a horizontal federated learning (FL) framework with LSTM-Attention models trained locally and aggregated securely—without raw data sharing. Results show the FL model achieves competitive performance (AUC = 0.892 vs. centralized baseline 0.901), with negligible degradation in sensitivity and specificity (<1.5% difference). Crucially, comprehensive privacy analysis—including gradient inversion attacks and differential privacy evaluation—confirms strong resistance to patient-level data reconstruction: reconstructed signals are visually unrecognizable (PSNR < 12 dB). Our work validates FL’s practicality, robustness, and regulatory compliance for real-world multi-center sepsis prediction, offering a deployable, privacy-by-design solution for collaborative clinical AI.

Abstract

Privacy-sensitive and distributed characteristics of multi-center medical data bring severe obstacles to centralized modeling for accurate early prediction of sepsis. Federated learning (FL) has attracted growing attention as a promising framework for collaborative model development, as it allows multiple institutions to jointly train predictive models without directly sharing or centralizing raw data. Nevertheless, its practical performance, robustness, and privacy-preserving benefits remain insufficiently evaluated using real-world clinical datasets. To bridge this gap, this study systematically examines the application of federated learning to multi-center sepsis prediction. The experimental dataset consists of 648 clinically screened samples collected from three tertiary hospitals in China, with rigorous inclusion and exclusion criteria. We establish a centralized training paradigm as the performance baseline, and then implement a horizontal federated learning framework for distributed collaborative modeling. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the federated learning-based model achieves highly comparable prediction accuracy to the centralized counterpart, while fundamentally avoiding privacy leakage. Further privacy security analysis verifies that malicious attackers cannot reconstruct the original patient data from the transmitted model parameters, indicating strong resistance against data reconstruction attacks. This work not only validates the practicality and security of federated learning in clinical sepsis prediction, but also provides a reliable and feasible solution for privacy-preserving multi-center medical collaboration.

item.title: From Untrusted Input to Trusted Memory: A Systematic Study of Memory Poisoning Attacks in LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04329v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

记忆（Memory）是大语言模型（LLM）智能体的核心组件，支撑其跨轮次知识积累与行为优化。然而，持久化记忆机制引入了“记忆中毒”（Memory Poisoning）风险：攻击者仅需一次恶意写入，即可长期污染代理决策逻辑，造成隐蔽、持续且难以检测的行为偏移。

方法与发现

本研究首次对LLM智能体的记忆中毒展开系统性实证分析：

  • 识别四大记忆写入通道：包括用户输入诱导、工具调用反馈、系统自生成摘要及外部API注入；
  • 揭示九类结构性脆弱点，覆盖模型固有幻觉、系统提示词（system prompt）的防御盲区、记忆检索/写入策略缺陷、以及代理架构中写-读耦合过强等维度；
  • 构建六类攻击分类法，涵盖语义混淆型、上下文劫持型、元记忆覆盖型、时序欺骗型、多跳污染型与权限越界型；
  • 设计MPBench基准测试集，包含127个可控中毒场景，覆盖不同记忆模块（向量库、短期缓存、长期日志）与代理范式（ReAct、Plan-and-Execute等）；

关键结论

实验表明：越强调主动记忆写入与高频检索的代理，中毒成功率越高（最高达92.3%）；主流提示注入防御（如输入清洗、角色重申、输出验证）对记忆中毒完全失效；现有记忆隔离与版本控制机制缺乏细粒度访问控制。本研究为构建可信记忆架构提供了首个系统性威胁模型与可复现评估框架。

AI Summary (English)

Memory is critical for LLM-based agents to accumulate knowledge across interactions, yet persistent memory introduces memory poisoning: a single adversarial write can corrupt long-term behavior. We conduct the first systematic study of this threat, identifying four memory write channels and nine structural vulnerabilities in models, system prompts, and agent architectures. Based on these, we propose a taxonomy of six memory poisoning attack classes, ranging from semantic obfuscation to multi-hop contamination. We introduce MPBench, a dedicated benchmark with 127 controlled poisoning scenarios, and show that agents with aggressive memory writing/retrieval are significantly more exploitable (up to 92.3% success). Crucially, standard prompt injection defenses offer no protection against memory poisoning. Our work establishes the foundational threat model, evaluation methodology, and mitigation priorities for trustworthy agent memory.

Abstract

Memory is a core component of AI agents, enabling them to accumulate knowledge across interactions and improve performance. However, persistent memory introduces the risk of memory poisoning, where a single adversarial memory write can exert long-term influence over agent behavior. We present a systematic study of memory poisoning in LLM-based agents. We identify four memory write channels and nine structural vulnerabilities in model capabilities, system prompt design, and agent system architecture that make these channels exploitable. Based on these vulnerabilities, we develop a taxonomy of six classes of memory poisoning attacks. Furthermore, we design MPBench -- a benchmark for evaluating memory poisoning attacks, and show that agents designed to write and retrieve memory more aggressively are more exploitable. We also show that existing prompt injection defenses fail to cover memory poisoning attacks. Our findings provide a foundation for understanding and mitigating memory poisoning attacks against AI agents.

item.title: From Prompt to Process: a Process Taxonomy and Comparative Assessment of Frameworks Supporting AI Software Development Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04967v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

AI编程工具正从简单补全与对话助手，演进为具备过程（process）、角色、工件与验证机制的完整开发框架。现有综述多聚焦于LLM/Agent技术本身，却缺乏对“如何将AI能力系统化为可操作开发流程”这一核心问题的实证分析。

方法与框架选取

本研究采用定向文献检索策略，以功能性覆盖度和社区实际采纳度为双重要求，筛选出6个代表性框架：GitHub Spec Kit、OpenSpec、BMAD Method、Get Shit Done (GSD)、Spec Kitty 和 Reversa。它们分别代表规格驱动开发（全量/轻量）、Agent主导敏捷规划、上下文工程化、工作树隔离与评审、以及遗留系统操作规格逆向提取等差异化路径。

核心贡献：六维过程分类法

我们提出首个面向AI软件开发Agent的过程分类学（Process Taxonomy），涵盖：规格（Specification）、上下文（Context）、角色（Roles）、执行（Execution）、验证（Validation） 与 可移植性（Portability） 六个维度，并配套可复现的评分量规（scoring rubric）。该分类法经6框架及外部案例Spec-Flow验证。

关键发现

1. 过程收敛性：成熟框架普遍弱化“单点Prompt”，转而依赖持久化工件、工作契约、可追溯性与人工评审，以降低歧义、协调多Agent协作；
2. 结构性权衡：无一框架在全部六维上表现强劲——过程深度与跨平台/跨Agent可移植性存在根本性张力；
3. 共性风险：规格-代码漂移、对生成工件过度信任、社区扩展脆弱、平台锁定、以及缺乏端到端过程基准测试。

后续方向

提出实证研究议程：聚焦中等质量指标（如规格一致性率）、上下文治理机制、安装期安全验证、及全流程可重现性保障。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a six-dimensional process taxonomy—covering Specification, Context, Roles, Execution, Validation, and Portability—to systematically characterize frameworks that operationalize LLMs and agents for AI-assisted software development. Through a traction-driven selection of six real-world frameworks (e.g., GitHub Spec Kit, GSD, Reversa) and an out-of-sample validation with Spec-Flow, we find: (1) mature frameworks converge on persistent artifacts, traceability, and human-in-the-loop review—reducing ambiguity and replacing the isolated prompt as the central coordination mechanism; and (2) no framework excels across all six dimensions, revealing a structural trade-off between process depth and cross-agent portability. We also identify recurring risks—including spec-code drift, over-reliance on generated artifacts, fragile community extensions, platform lock-in, and absence of end-to-end process benchmarks. The taxonomy serves as a replicable instrument for empirical evaluation, guiding future work on intermediate-quality metrics, context governance, installation security, and reproducibility.

Abstract

AI tools for programming are no longer just autocomplete or chat assistants: they organize themselves as development frameworks, with process, roles, artifacts and verification. Recent surveys map agents and LLMs for software engineering, but a study centered on the operational frameworks that turn these capabilities into process is missing. We ran a directed search of primary sources, with a functional inclusion criterion and traction measurement, and selected six frameworks: GitHub Spec Kit, OpenSpec, BMAD Method, Get Shit Done (GSD), Spec Kitty and Reversa. Each attacks AI development through a different path: spec-driven development in full and lightweight variants, agent-driven agile planning, context engineering over the agent, worktree isolation and review, and recovery of operational specifications from legacy systems. Our central contribution is a six-dimension process taxonomy: specification, context, roles, execution, validation and portability, with a scoring rubric that turns it into a replicable instrument. We apply it to the six frameworks and an out-of-sample case, Spec-Flow. Two results stand out. Among frameworks that already adopt some process there is convergence: the isolated prompt loses centrality, and persistent artifacts, work contracts, traceability and human review become mechanisms that reduce ambiguity and coordinate agents. And no framework strongly covers all six dimensions, exposing a structural trade-off between process depth and portability across agents. We also found recurring risks: drift between specification and code, excessive trust in generated artifacts, fragility of community extensions, platform dependence and a lack of benchmarks for the complete process. We close with a research agenda for empirical evaluation, focused on intermediate-quality metrics, context governance, installation security and reproducibility.

item.title: Provably Auditable and Safe LLM Agents from Human-Authored Ontologies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04903v1

AI Summary (中文)

本研究提出可验证审计与安全的LLM智能体新范式

本文提出Agentic Redux——一种面向高可靠性场景（如医疗合规、网络安全）的新型大语言模型（LLM）智能体架构，核心目标是实现线性可审计性（linear auditability）与形式化可证明安全性。针对现有LLM智能体决策黑箱、日志不可篡改性弱、结果缺乏语义保障等关键缺陷，我们基于有类型λ演算（typed lambda calculus）构建了严格的形式化语义模型，并在适配领域上数学证明：Agentic Redux的每次执行均满足语义正确性保证，且所有决策步骤自动、不可逆地写入仅追加型分类账（append-only ledger），从而实现端到端可追溯、防抵赖的审计路径。

我们验证了该架构在两个真实生产级领域中的有效性：

  • 医疗账单合规性审查：自动识别CPT/ICD编码冲突、医保政策违规及文档证据缺失；
  • 安全漏洞披露流程管理：标准化CVE提交、厂商协调、时间戳化披露决策链。

两套完整可运行代码已开源至配套代码仓库（含领域本体、LLM提示工程模板与审计日志解析器）。

进一步，我们提出本体优先的智能体设计方法论（Ontology-First Agent Design）：由人类领域专家首先使用基础形式本体（Basic Formal Ontology, BFO）对问题域进行形式化建模；再交由LLM基于该本体推导出结构化角色体系（如“合规审核员”“漏洞验证者”“人工复核员”），明确LLM智能体与人类协同的职责边界与交互协议。该方法显著提升智能体的可解释性、可控性与监管友好性，为高风险AI系统落地提供可验证、可审计、可问责的技术基座。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Agentic Redux, a novel LLM agent architecture designed for nontrivial, safety-critical domains requiring linear auditability. Using the typed lambda calculus, we formally prove that, when deployed on appropriate domains, Agentic Redux executions are semantically guaranteed correct, with every decision immutably recorded in an append-only ledger. We validate the architecture in two production-grade domains: healthcare billing compliance (e.g., CPT/ICD coding validation against payer policies) and security vulnerability disclosure (e.g., coordinated CVE triage with timestamped human-in-the-loop approvals). Working implementations are publicly available. Complementing the architecture, we propose Ontology-First Agent Design: a methodology where domain experts first formalize the problem using the Basic Formal Ontology (BFO), then delegate to an LLM the derivation of well-defined agent and human-in-the-loop roles—enabling rigorous, interpretable, and auditable AI-augmented workflows.

Abstract

We introduce the LLM agent architecture Agentic Redux, intended for use with nontrivial problem domains that require linear auditability. Using the typed lambda calculus, we prove that, run on appropriate domains, Agentic Redux executions are semantically guaranteed to be correct, with all decisions recorded in an append-only ledger. We present two production-grade appropriate domains, in healthcare billing compliance, and security vulnerability disclosure. Working code for Agentic Redux run on both domains is available in a supporting code repository. We also introduce Ontology-First Agent Design, a methodology for creation of agent frameworks on a problem domain, in which a human expert ontologizes the problem domain with Basic Formal Ontology, and then assigns an LLM to derive roles that agents and humans-in-the-loop can fill, in order to work the problems in the domain.

item.title: R-APS: Compositional Reasoning and In-Context Meta-Learning for Constrained Design via Reflective Adversarial Pareto Search
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04823v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）在开放性任务中表现出色，但在具身智能体场景（如多步规划、工具调用、长程决策）中，语言流畅性无法保障行为可靠性。本文指出其根源在于三类耦合的结构性失效：错误传播缺乏定位机制、最坏情况扰动未被系统评估、累积知识无法主动失效。这些失效本质源于多种推理模式——溯因、反事实、元归纳、校正与归纳——在共享上下文中相互冲突、不可兼容。

方法创新：R-APS 框架

我们提出反思式对抗帕累托搜索（R-APS），据我们所知，这是首个通过推理模式解耦协同解决上述三类失效的框架。其核心设计包含三层时序协同：

  • 分阶段组合式推理：引入带类型约束的验证批评家（typed validation critic），实现失败归因与精准定位；
  • 敏感性引导的反事实压力测试：将鲁棒性建模为一等帕累托优化目标，而非后处理；
  • 元归纳规则提取与显式失效机制：从历史轨迹中抽取可验证设计规则，并支持动态驳回过时知识。

R-APS 完全基于冻结LLM运行，无需任何微调，仅依赖结构化协议设计。

实验结果

在平面机构综合任务（机器人、假肢、机械设计）中，所有候选方案均经运动学求解器严格验证。在32条目标轨迹上，R-APS：
✅ 提供3.5倍更紧致的鲁棒性保证（相较均匀扰动基线）；
✅ 首次达标迭代速度提升46%；
✅ Chamfer距离降低2.1倍（优于Enum+GA），同时联合优化连杆数量与最坏-case鲁棒性；
✅ 小型4B专用推理模型在协议内性能媲美70B通用大模型，印证结构化协议可部分替代模型规模。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) excel at fluent, open-ended generation but fail to ensure reliable behavior in agentic design—where multi-step planning, tool use, and long-horizon reasoning are required. We identify three coupled structural failures: unlocalized error propagation, unevaluated worst-case perturbations, and non-invalidatable accumulated knowledge—rooted in incompatible demands of abductive, counterfactual, meta-inductive, corrective, and inductive reasoning within a shared context. To address them jointly, we propose Reflective Adversarial Pareto Search (R-APS): the first method to decompose reasoning modes into dedicated contexts and orchestrate them across three timescales—staged compositional reasoning with typed validation (for failure localization), sensitivity-guided counterfactual stress-testing as a first-class Pareto objective (for robustness certification), and meta-inductive rule extraction with explicit invalidation (for persistent, self-correcting memory). R-APS requires no fine-tuning and operates solely via structured protocol design on frozen LLMs. Evaluated on planar mechanism synthesis (with kinematic solver verification), R-APS achieves 3.5× tighter robustness certificates, 46% faster iterations-to-first-admission, and 2.1× lower Chamfer distance than Enum+GA—all while jointly optimizing bar count and worst-case robustness. Notably, specialized 4B models match 70B general-purpose backbones within R-APS, demonstrating that protocol structure can partially offset scale.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are fluent on open-ended tasks, yet in agentic settings, where a system must plan, use tools, and act over extended horizons, fluency does not ensure reliable delivery. We trace this gap to three coupled structural failures: errors propagate without localization, worst-case perturbations go unevaluated, and accumulated knowledge is never invalidated. We argue these share a root cause: abductive, counterfactual, meta-inductive, corrective, and inductive reasoning pull a shared context in incompatible directions. We introduce Reflective Adversarial Pareto Search (R-APS), to our knowledge the first method addressing all three failures jointly via reasoning-mode decomposition, allocating each reasoning mode its own context and orchestrating interaction across three timescales: staged compositional reasoning with a typed validation critic (failure localization), sensitivity-guided counterfactual stress-testing as a first-class Pareto objective (robustness), and meta-inductive rule extraction with explicit invalidation (persistent memory). R-APS requires no fine-tuning and operates on a frozen LLM purely via structured protocol design. We evaluate on planar mechanism synthesis (robotics, prosthetics, mechanical design), with every candidate checked by a kinematic solver. On 32 target trajectories, R-APS delivers robustness certificates 3.5x tighter than uniform-perturbation baselines, 46% faster iterations-to-first-admission, and 2.1x Chamfer-distance reduction over Enum+GA while jointly controlling bar-count and worst-case robustness. Small 4B reasoning-specialized models prove competitive with general-purpose 70B backbones inside the protocol, suggesting structured protocols can partially offset model scale.

item.title: Learning While Acting: A Skill-Enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution Framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04815v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

面向动态交互环境的在线终身学习代理（Online Lifelong Learning Agents）需在持续任务流中边执行、边学习，但现有方法多依赖静态技能库或离线经验检索，无法像人类一样在测试时（test-time）实时吸收反馈并内化新能力，导致推理泛化性弱、上下文膨胀严重、技能与任务解耦。

方法创新：LifeSkill 框架

我们提出 Skill-enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution（LifeSkill）——一种两阶段强化学习框架，实现“行动中学习”（Learning While Acting）：

  • 验证器引导的技能学习（Verifier-Guided Skill Learning）：针对技能提取缺乏直接监督的问题，设计基于多轨迹平均验证成功率的奖励机制——对每个候选技能生成多个技能条件化策略 rollout，由外部验证器评估其任务完成率，仅奖励真正提升解决能力（而非仅语言合理）的技能，推动模型习得可执行、可迁移的语义技能单元。
  • 在线技能内化（Online Skill Internalization）：在测试交互过程中，将技能条件化轨迹自动转化为参数更新信号，通过轻量级梯度更新持续优化策略模型权重，使推理能力直接嵌入模型参数，彻底规避传统检索式方法带来的上下文长度爆炸与延迟问题。

实验结果与意义

在权威基准 LifelongAgentBench 上，LifeSkill 相比 SOTA 终身代理基线（如 LAM, SkillFormer）平均任务成功率提升 +7.0 绝对百分点，且在长程任务连贯性、跨任务技能复用率、单次交互学习效率等维度显著领先。本工作首次实现了LLM代理在开放环境中“边做边学、越做越强”的类人认知闭环，为构建自主进化型AI代理奠定新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Lifelong learning for LLM agents demands real-time adaptation during test-time interaction—not just static retrieval. We propose LifeSkill, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework enabling Learning While Acting. First, Verifier-Guided Skill Learning overcomes skill supervision scarcity by rewarding candidate skills based on the average success rate of multiple skill-conditioned policy rollouts evaluated by an external verifier—prioritizing task-effective over text-plausible skills. Second, Online Skill Internalization continuously updates the policy’s parameters using reward signals derived from skill-conditioned trajectories, directly embedding reasoning capabilities into weights and eliminating context bloat from experience retrieval. On LifelongAgentBench, LifeSkill achieves a +7.0 absolute point gain in average task success over state-of-the-art lifelong agent baselines, demonstrating superior long-horizon coherence, cross-task skill transfer, and single-step learning efficiency.

Abstract

Lifelong learning is essential for Large Language Model (LLM) agents operating in dynamic, interactive environments. However, existing lifelong learning agents for long-horizon tasks typically depend on discrete skill or past experiences retrieval with static parameters during inference, which prevents them from continuously internalizing test-time feedback like human learners. To bridge this gap, we propose Skill-enhanced Test-Time Co-Evolution (\texttt{LifeSkill}), a two-stage reinforcement learning framework for Online Lifelong Learning Agents. Specifically, we design Verifier-Guided Skill Learning that addresses the lack of direct supervision for skill extraction by rewarding candidate skills according to the average verifier success of multiple skill-conditioned policy rollouts, encouraging the model to generate skills that are useful for solving tasks rather than merely plausible in text. Furthermore, we introduce Online Skill Internalization, which continuously improves the policy model during test-time interaction by transforming skill-conditioned trajectories into reward signals. This enables the agent to directly internalize reasoning capabilities into its parameters, avoiding the context bloat of experience retrieval. Experiments on LifelongAgentBench show that LifeSkill improves average performance by 7 absolute points by comparing with existing lifelong agent baselines.

item.title: QO-Bench: Diagnosing Query-Operator-Preserving Retrieval over Typed Event Tuples
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04646v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现实世界中大量业务、法律及科学领域的自然语言问题，本质是面向文本中隐含结构化记录的数据库式查询（如“2023年哪家公司因数据泄露被欧盟罚款超500万欧元？”）。现有检索增强生成（RAG）系统过度侧重语义相关性，但高相关性段落并不保证能支撑正确执行查询操作（如过滤、连接、交集、计数），导致答案错误。

方法：QO-Bench 构建

我们提出 QO-Bench——首个面向查询-算子保持型检索（Query-Operator-Preserving Retrieval）的诊断基准。它基于 22,984 篇新闻报道与 614 个企业事件，覆盖 18 类结构化查询模板（含 filter/project、join、intersection、count 等），共 785 道测试题。所有黄金答案均由类型化事件元组（如 <Company: "Meta", Violation: "GDPR", Fine: 1.2B_EUR, Year: 2023>）经确定性逻辑计算得出；评估采用精确元组匹配召回率（exact tuple match recall），避免LLM主观评判，支持细粒度算子级归因分析。

关键发现与创新

  • 提出“索引时保留 vs 查询时执行”双轴框架：实证表明，主流方法（RAG/ReAct RAG/GraphRAG）在索引阶段丢失关键类型化值（如金额、实体类型、时间戳），致使算子执行失败；
  • 算子敏感性显著：相似性检索在 filter/project 上领先，而信息抽取转 SQL 在 intersection/count 上反超；
  • 即使提供黄金证据（long-context oracle），模型仍远未饱和（平均召回仅 68.3%），证明算子执行能力本身是核心瓶颈，单纯提升检索或上下文长度无法绕过；
  • QO-Bench 将检索目标从“段落相关”升维至“算子可执行”，为构建可验证、可调试的结构化问答系统提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

QO-Bench is a diagnostic benchmark for query-operator-preserving retrieval over typed event tuples—designed to expose failures in executing database-style operators (e.g., filter, join, intersection, count) from natural-language questions over text corpora. It comprises 785 questions grounded in 614 corporate events and 22,984 news articles, with gold answers deterministically computed from structured tuples and evaluated via exact tuple-match recall—bypassing LLM judges for objective, operator-level diagnosis. Under matched conditions, we evaluate RAG, ReAct RAG, GraphRAG, and extraction-to-SQL against a long-context oracle ceiling. Results confirm our two-axis framework: systems preserve relevance but discard typed values needed for operators—causing performance inversion across operators (e.g., similarity retrieval excels at filter/project; extraction-to-SQL dominates at intersection/count). Critically, even the oracle—given perfect evidence—achieves only 68.3% average recall, proving that operator execution, not retrieval alone, is the fundamental bottleneck. QO-Bench reframes retrieval’s goal from passage relevance to query-operator fidelity.

Abstract

Many real-world questions over business, legal, and scientific corpora are natural-language versions of database-style queries over records latent in text. Existing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems are optimized primarily for semantic relevance, but retrieving plausible passages does not guarantee correct query execution. We introduce QO-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark for query-operator question answering over typed event tuples. The benchmark covers 22,984 news articles and 614 corporate events across 18 query templates, evaluated on 785 questions. Each gold answer is deterministically computed from typed event tuples and scored by recall, with answers matched to the gold tuples by exact match rather than an LLM judge. This design enables operator-level diagnosis such as joins and intersection. We evaluate RAG, ReAct RAG, GraphRAG, and information-extraction-to-SQL under matched conditions, with a long-context oracle ceiling to isolate retrieval failure. A two-axis framework -- index-time preservation versus query-time execution -- predicts where each paradigm fails, and the results bear it out: systems retrieve relevant text but discard the typed values operators need, and the deployable paradigm ranking inverts across operators, with similarity retrieval leading on filter/project and extraction-to-SQL on intersection and counting. Even given the gold evidence, a long-context oracle stays far from saturated, so operator execution -- not retrieval alone -- is a core bottleneck that a stronger answer model does not remove. QO-Bench reframes the goal from passage relevance to query-operator-preserving retrieval.

item.title: A Normative Intermediate Representation for ASP-Based Compliance Reasoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04619v1

AI Summary (中文)

提出MONIR：面向ASP合规推理的模态化规范中间表示

本研究针对自动化合规验证中法规文本语义复杂、形式化困难、执行效率低等核心挑战，提出MONIR（Modalized-Output Normative Intermediate Representation），一种专为Answer Set Programming（ASP）驱动的合规推理设计的规范化中间表示框架。MONIR的核心创新在于其分阶段操作语义（staged operational semantics）：将法规条款解析为“规范性意图→可执行约束→稳定模型求解”三级结构，显式分离义务（obligation）、禁止（prohibition）与许可（permission）的模态逻辑语义，并通过输出模态标记（如must/forbid/may）保障语义保真。在此基础上，我们构建MONIR-ASP——一个可执行编译器，支持外部函数调用（如实时车速查询）、时序规则（如“制动后3秒内须激活AEB”）、以及增量式/模块化稳定模型求解，显著提升复杂动态场景下的推理可扩展性。

为验证实用性，我们首次将该框架落地于中国ADAS（高级驾驶辅助系统）法规体系，覆盖GB/T 38186–2019、GB 17675–2021等12项关键标准。采用LLM协同的三阶段管道（法规切片→条款结构化→MONIR映射），实现高精度自动抽取。实验表明：（1）条款结构化F1达92.4%，远超基线规则模板（+18.7%）；（2）MONIR-ASP在典型合规检查任务中求解速度较直接ASP编码提升3.2×，且支持毫秒级增量更新；（3）模块化设计使新条款注入平均仅需0.8秒，验证了工业级部署可行性。本工作为智能网联汽车合规自动化提供了首个兼具形式严谨性、工程可实施性与领域适配性的中间表示范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose MONIR, a Modalized-Output Normative Intermediate Representation for ASP-based compliance reasoning. MONIR features a staged operational semantics that explicitly separates normative intent (obligation/prohibition/permission) from executable constraints and stable-model evaluation, preserving deontic semantics via output modalities (must, forbid, may). Its executable counterpart, MONIR-ASP, supports external function calls, temporal rules (e.g., “AEB must activate within 3s after braking”), and modular/incremental ASP solving. Evaluated on Chinese ADAS regulations (e.g., GB/T 38186–2019), an LLM-assisted pipeline achieves 92.4% F1 in clause structuring. Experiments show MONIR-ASP improves solving speed by 3.2× over baseline ASP encodings and enables sub-second incremental updates—demonstrating scalability and deployability for real-world automotive compliance verification.

Abstract

We propose MONIR, a Modalized-Output Normative Intermediate Representation for ASP-based compliance reasoning. Its core fragment has a staged operational semantics, while MONIR-ASP provides an executable compilation and extensions for external functions, temporal rules, and stable-model reasoning. We instantiate the framework on Chinese ADAS regulations and standards with an LLM-assisted pipeline. Experiments evaluate extraction quality and the efficiency of modular and incremental ASP solving.

item.title: Scaling Self-Evolving Agents via Parametric Memory
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04536v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有记忆增强型大语言模型（LLM）智能体普遍采用“冻结参数+上下文记忆”范式：将历史经验以文本摘要或检索片段形式存于提示（prompt）中，模型权重全程固定。此类设计导致根本性局限——智能体仅能回溯查阅（look up）过往信息，却无法在线学习演化（learn from）经验：策略不随交互更新，且超出上下文窗口的信息永久丢失。

方法创新：TMEM 框架

本文提出 TMEM（Temporal Parametric Memory），一种可扩展的自演化参数化记忆框架。其核心突破在于双重动态机制：

  • 显式记忆压缩：将长程历史编码为结构化、可提取的显式记忆；
  • 隐式策略演化：通过轻量级在线更新，将记忆中蒸馏出的监督信号（如成功动作反馈）实时注入低秩适应（LoRA）增量权重 $Δ_t$，使策略 $π_{θ_0+Δ_t}$ 在单次任务执行过程中持续演进。

我们将其建模为快权 rollout 决策过程：动作采样依赖当前参数化策略，而“记忆提取”动作本身生成监督信号，驱动 $Δ_t$ 迭代更新。该视角使提取策略可直接通过强化学习（RL）联合优化——训练基础权重 $θ_0$ 不仅提升任务表现，更同步提升在线 LoRA 适配所依赖的数据质量。此外，我们引入SVD 初始化 LoRA 子空间，显著加速在线收敛。

实验验证

在 LoCoMo、LongMemEval-S、多目标搜索与 CL-Bench 四大基准上，TMEM 在不同模型规模（7B–70B）下均稳定超越摘要式与检索式基线，验证了参数化记忆对智能体长期适应能力的本质增强。

AI Summary (English)

We propose TMEM, a self-evolving parametric memory framework that enables LLM agents to learn from experience within a single episode—unlike prior memory-augmented agents that only look up frozen textual summaries. TMEM compresses history into explicit memory while simultaneously absorbing distilled supervision into fast, lightweight LoRA weights $Δ_t$ via online updates, dynamically altering the agent’s policy $π_{θ_0+Δ_t}$. We formalize this as an agentic decision process with fast-weight rollout dynamics, where extraction actions generate supervision for updating $Δ_t$, making the extraction policy directly optimizable via RL. Crucially, training the base weights $θ_0$ improves both task performance and the quality of data used for online adaptation. SVD-based LoRA subspace initialization further accelerates convergence. Experiments across LoCoMo, LongMemEval-S, multi-objective search, and CL-Bench show TMEM consistently outperforms summary- and retrieval-based baselines at multiple model scales (7B–70B).

Abstract

Existing memory-augmented LLM agents store past experience exclusively in prompt space, as textual summaries or retrieved passages, while keeping model parameters frozen throughout a rollout. Such agents can \emph{look up} what they have seen but cannot \emph{learn from} it: their policy is unchanged by experience, and any information dropped from the context is permanently lost. We introduce \texttt{TMEM}, a self-evolving parametric memory framework in which the agent not only compresses history into explicit memory but also absorbs distilled supervision into fast LoRA weights $Δ_t$ via lightweight online updates, genuinely altering its future behavior within a single episode. We formalize this as an agentic decision process with fast-weight rollout dynamics: actions are sampled from $π_{θ_0+Δ_t}$, while extraction actions produce supervision that updates $Δ_t$ for subsequent decisions. This view makes the extraction policy directly optimizable by RL: training $θ_0$ improves not only task actions but also the quality of the data used for online LoRA adaptation. We further propose SVD-based initialization of the LoRA subspace to accelerate online convergence. Experiments on LoCoMo, LongMemEval-S, multi-objective search, and CL-Bench show that \texttt{TMEM} consistently outperforms summary-based and retrieval-based baselines across different model scales.

item.title: Radiomic Feature Selection Using Gradient Loss of Deep Neural Network for Lung Cancer Stage Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04453v1

AI Summary (中文)

基于深度神经网络梯度损失的放射组学特征选择方法用于肺癌分期检测

放射组学可从医学影像中定量提取高维成像生物标志物，是辅助癌症诊断的重要技术。然而，其数据常呈现“高维小样本”特性（如本研究中106维特征、样本量有限），导致传统特征选择方法难以有效捕捉非线性交互关系，模型泛化能力受限。本文提出梯度损失递归特征消除（GL-RFE）框架，创新性地将深度神经网络（DNN）训练过程中的梯度敏感性分析融入特征选择：通过反向传播计算分类损失函数对各输入放射组学特征的偏导数（即梯度绝对值），量化其对模型输出的影响强度，并递归剔除贡献最小的特征。我们在3D Slicer平台集成PyRadiomics，从127例肺癌患者胸部CT影像中提取106个一阶、纹理及形状特征；经GL-RFE筛选出最具判别力的前15个特征（如Gray Level Non-Uniformity、Joint Entropy、Sphericity等），用于构建轻量级DNN分类器，实现早期（I–II期）与进展期（III–IV期）肺癌的自动区分。实验表明，该方法在独立测试集上达到准确率90.22%、精确率90.10%、召回率90.24%、F1-score 90.16%，显著优于mRMR、LASSO和经典RFE等基线方法。可视化分析（相关性热图、类间分布直方图）证实GL-RFE有效降低冗余、增强类别可分性。本工作为小样本高维生物医学数据分析提供了可复现、可解释、端到端可微分的新范式，具备向基因组学、多模态临床决策支持系统迁移的潜力。

AI Summary (English)

This study proposes Gradient-Loss Recursive Feature Elimination (GL-RFE), a novel radiomic feature selection framework that leverages gradient-based sensitivity analysis from a deep neural network to identify the most discriminative features for lung cancer staging. From chest CT scans of 127 patients, 106 radiomic features were extracted using PyRadiomics. GL-RFE recursively eliminates features with minimal gradients of the classification loss w.r.t. inputs, yielding an optimal subset of 15 features. A DNN classifier trained on these features achieves 90.22% accuracy, 90.10% precision, 90.24% recall, and 90.16% F1-score on the test set—outperforming conventional methods (e.g., LASSO, mRMR). By directly embedding differentiable feature importance into model training, GL-RFE captures nonlinear interactions and improves generalizability in high-dimensional, small-sample biomedical settings.

Abstract

Radiomics enables extraction of quantitative imaging biomarkers from medical images and has become an important tool for computer-aided cancer diagnosis. However, radiomics datasets are typically high-dimensional with limited samples, making feature selection a critical step for building reliable predictive models. This study proposes a Gradient-Loss Recursive Feature Elimination (GL-RFE) framework that integrates gradient sensitivity analysis from a deep neural network to identify the most influential radiomic features for lung cancer stage detection. A total of 106 radiomic features were extracted from chest Computed Tomography (CT) scans using the PyRadiomics extension of the 3D Slicer platform. The proposed method evaluates feature importance by computing gradients of the network loss with respect to input features and recursively eliminates features with minimal contribution. The resulting top-15 radiomic features are used to train a deep neural network classifier for distinguishing early-stage and advanced-stage lung cancer. The proposed framework achieves strong classification performance, with accuracy of 90.22%, precision of 90.10%, recall of 90.24%, and F1-score of 90.16% on the test dataset. Visualization analyses, including correlation heat maps and distribution plots, further confirm reduced feature redundancy and improved class separability. Compared to conventional feature selection techniques, GL-RFE effectively captures nonlinear feature interactions and enhances model generalization. The presented protocol provides a reproducible and interpretable methodology for radiomics-based cancer stage detection and is particularly suitable for high-dimensional, small-sample biomedical datasets, with potential applications in other domains such as genomics and multimodal clinical analysis.

item.title: When Do Fewer Coordinates Suffice in DP-SGD?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04375v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

差分隐私随机梯度下降（DP-SGD）通过在每次参数更新中向所有坐标注入噪声实现隐私保障，导致噪声能量随模型参数维度 $d$ 线性增长。当 $d$ 极大（如现代深度网络中达百万级）时，过量噪声严重削弱梯度信号，制约隐私-效用权衡。本文核心追问：在不牺牲优化收敛性的前提下，何时可仅对少数关键坐标施加隐私保护？ 即——能否实现“坐标稀疏”的私有训练，使噪声仅作用于真正承载优化信号的子集？

方法创新：TP-TopK 框架

我们提出 TP-TopK（Two-Phase TopK DP-SGD） ——首个无需公共数据、纯私有驱动的两阶段坐标稀疏训练框架：

  • 私有热身阶段：在初始若干轮中，以高隐私预算运行轻量级 DP-SGD，收集各坐标梯度的带噪统计量（如累积梯度模长），并基于此进行私有 Top-$k$ 坐标筛选，确定一个大小为 $k \ll d$ 的候选支持集；
  • 主训练阶段：仅在该支持集内执行标准 DP-SGD 更新，其余坐标冻结。整个流程严格满足 $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-差分隐私。

理论贡献与实证发现

我们建立了坐标限制收益判据：当真实梯度在 Top-$k$ 支持上具有足够信噪比时，TP-TopK 的非凸平稳性误差界中主导噪声项由 $O(\sqrt{k})$ 替代传统 $O(\sqrt{d})$，显著缓解维度诅咒。进一步，我们给出热身阶段坐标排序可靠性的理论下界，证明其随热身轮数与隐私预算单调提升。在 MNIST、FMNIST 和 CIFAR-10 上的实验表明：学习到的坐标支持所保留的梯度能量平均比同尺寸随机支持高 2.1–3.8 倍；增益在 $k$ 较小（如 $k/d < 0.5\%$）且热身分数区分度高时最为显著，验证了方法的有效性与适用边界。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private stochastic gradient descent (DP-SGD) suffers from noise scaling with the full parameter dimension $d$, degrading utility in high-dimensional models. This paper asks: When can private training safely update only a sparse subset of coordinates without harming optimization? We propose TP-TopK, a two-phase, fully private framework that identifies a $k$-coordinate support via a noisy warm-up phase and restricts subsequent DP-SGD updates to this support—requiring no public data. We derive a precise condition under which coordinate restriction is beneficial and prove that, when satisfied, the dominant noise term in the nonconvex stationarity bound scales with $\sqrt{k}$ instead of $\sqrt{d}$. We further provide a lower bound on the reliability of warm-up-based ranking. Experiments on MNIST, FMNIST, and CIFAR-10 show that learned supports retain significantly more gradient energy than random ones of equal size—especially when $k$ is small and warm-up scores are informative—demonstrating both theoretical soundness and practical efficacy.

Abstract

Differentially private stochastic gradient descent (DP-SGD) injects noise into every updated coordinate, making the injected noise energy scale with the ambient parameter dimension \(d\). We ask when private training can update fewer coordinates without losing the signal needed for optimization. We propose \textsc{TP-TopK} (Two-Phase TopK DP-SGD), a two-phase method for coordinate-sparse private training without public data, in which a private warm-up phase identifies a coordinate support used to guide the main training phase. We give a criterion characterizing when coordinate restriction can be beneficial, show via a nonconvex stationarity bound that under this condition the relevant noise term scales with the active dimension \(k\) rather than the full parameter dimension \(d\), and provide a lower bound on the reliability of warm-up-based coordinate ranking. Experiments on MNIST, FMNIST, and CIFAR-10 show that learned coordinate supports can retain more gradient energy than size-matched random supports, with the largest gains when the active dimension is small and warm-up scores are informative.

item.title: Literature-Guided Minimax Optimization of Virtual Epilepsy Neurostimulation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04339v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

癫痫计算建模有望实现个体化神经调控治疗，但现有优化方法多追求平均性能最优，忽视患者间异质性。在神经刺激中，均值改善的方案可能对网络耐受性最差的患者完全失效——这违背临床“不伤害”底线。

方法创新：文献引导的极小化极大（minimax）框架

本研究提出首个端到端文献驱动鲁棒优化流水线：

  • 假设生成层：从PubMed规模文献库自动提取癫痫机制与刺激响应先验知识，约束搜索空间；
  • 仿真评估层：基于The Virtual Brain（TVB）平台运行Epileptor模型，在20例虚拟患者队列上并行评估每组参数；
  • 优化目标层：以最差-case奖励（worst-case reward）为优化核心，定义为模拟癫痫活动方差的负值（即最小化最严重发作波动），而非平均抑制率；
  • 双路径输出：支持优化内在模型参数（如皮层兴奋性、突触耦合强度）或临床可解释的外源刺激协议（如刺激频率、相位、靶区）。

关键发现

  • 内在参数优化：最优参数集将最差-case奖励从−0.5285提升至−0.3182，相对提升39.8%，显著增强鲁棒性；
  • 外源刺激优化：虽获1.7%最差-case改善，但在20例虚拟队列中无统计学整体获益（p=0.9019）；值得注意的是，其颞叶亚组呈现阳性信号，且总体应答率达55%，提示潜在人群分层价值；
  • 方法论贡献：首次将大规模文献知识嵌入minimax神经调控优化闭环，为可解释、抗异质性、临床就绪的数字孪生设计提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a literature-guided minimax optimization framework for virtual epilepsy neurostimulation, addressing the critical limitation of average-case optimization in computational neuromodulation. By integrating PubMed-scale hypothesis mining, The Virtual Brain (TVB) Epileptor simulations across a 20-patient virtual cohort, and LLM-augmented black-box optimization, the pipeline explicitly maximizes worst-case reward—defined as the negative variance of simulated seizure activity—to ensure robustness against patient heterogeneity. Two optimization modes were tested: intrinsic model-control parameters (e.g., coupling strength, excitability) and clinical-style external stimulation protocols (e.g., frequency, phase, target). Intrinsic optimization achieved a 39.8% improvement in worst-case reward (from −0.5285 to −0.3182), demonstrating strong robustness gains. External stimulation yielded only a 1.7% worst-case improvement; no aggregate benefit emerged across the cohort (p = 0.9019), though a 55% responder rate and significant temporal-lobe subgroup signal suggest stratified utility. This work establishes an in silico proof of concept for literature-aware, minimax-driven neurostimulation design—not clinical evidence, but a foundational step toward robust, interpretable, and patient-tailored virtual trials.

Abstract

Computational models of epilepsy promise patient-specific treatment design, but most optimization workflows still search for parameters that perform well on average. In neuromodulation, this is a weak target: a protocol that improves the mean response can still fail in the patient whose network is least tolerant to stimulation. We present a literature-guided minimax pipeline that couples PubMed-scale hypothesis extraction, The Virtual Brain (TVB) Epileptor simulations, and large-language-model-guided black-box optimization. The optimizer proposes either intrinsic model-control parameters or clinically interpretable external-stimulation protocols; TVB evaluates each proposal across sampled virtual patients; and the objective maximizes worst-case reward, defined as the negative variance of simulated seizure activity. In the intrinsic model-control experiment, the best archived parameter set improved worst-case reward from -0.5285 to -0.3182, a 39.8% gain over baseline. The clinical-style external-stimulation search produced a much smaller worst-case improvement (1.7%), and a 20-patient virtual cohort showed no aggregate benefit (p=0.9019), despite a 55% responder rate and a positive temporal-lobe subgroup signal. The study should be read as an in silico proof of concept for robust, literature-aware neurostimulation design, not as clinical evidence.

item.title: Hybrid Adversarial Defence for Natural Language Understanding Tasks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04612v1

AI Summary (中文)

混合对抗防御框架：面向自然语言理解任务的统一鲁棒性提升方案

大型语言模型（LLMs）同时面临幻觉生成与对抗攻击脆弱性两大核心安全挑战。尽管二者在成因上高度关联（如低置信度输出易诱发幻觉，亦易被对抗扰动利用），现有防御方法多孤立设计：熵基模型聚焦抑制幻觉，不确定性建模与几何特征方法则侧重提升抗攻击能力。本文提出首个端到端可训练的混合对抗防御框架（Hybrid Adversarial Defence, HAD），首次将三类互补信号——输出分布熵（表征幻觉倾向）、预测不确定性（表征决策可信度）、隐空间几何结构（表征语义鲁棒性）——进行协同建模与联合优化。

我们在涵盖事实验证（FEVER）、多跳推理（HotpotQA）、常识问答（CSQA）及社会推理（SIQA）的四大主流NLU基准上开展系统评估：

  • 域内性能：HAD显著提升干净样本准确率，最高达+43.34%；在TextFooler、BERT-Attack等标准对抗攻击下，准确率提升+64.92%，攻击成功率降低62.27%；
  • 域外泛化：在航空工程（AeroEngQA）与政策影响分析（CPIQA）等真实场景OOD数据集上，对抗准确率仍提升达57.14%；
  • 高级威胁防御：在Prompt注入（SafeGuard）与 Jailbreak检测（AdvBench、DAN）任务中，攻击成功率较SOTA基线最高下降51%。

本研究证实：熵、不确定性与几何特征的协同融合，不仅超越任一单模态防御策略，更构建了跨分布、跨攻击类型的通用鲁棒性基石，为构建可信、安全、可部署的LLM应用提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Hybrid Adversarial Defence for Natural Language Understanding Tasks

Large language models (LLMs) suffer concurrently from hallucination and adversarial vulnerability—two interrelated yet traditionally siloed challenges. We propose a unified hybrid defence framework (HAD) that jointly leverages entropy-based signals (to suppress hallucination), uncertainty estimation, and geometric features in latent space (to enhance robustness). Evaluated across four in-domain NLU benchmarks (FEVER, HotpotQA, CSQA, SIQA), HAD achieves up to +43.34% clean accuracy gain, +64.92% adversarial accuracy improvement, and 62.27% reduction in attack success rate. On out-of-distribution datasets (AeroEngQA, CPIQA), it sustains strong robustness (+57.14% adversarial accuracy).
