Against prompt injection (SafeGuard) and jailbreak attacks (AdvBench, DAN), HAD reduces attack success by up to 51% over state-of-the-art baselines. Our results demonstrate that synergistic integration of entropy, uncertainty, and geometry yields significantly superior and generalizable defence—outperforming any single-feature approach across domains and attack types.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are vulnerable both to hallucination and adversarial manipulation. Although these problems are closely related, existing defences typically address them separately. We investigate a hybrid defence framework that combines entropy-based models, designed to reduce hallucinations, with uncertainty-based models and geometric-based models, designed to reduce vulnerability. Under in-domain tests on Natural Language Understanding datasets (FEVER, HotpotQA, CSQA, SIQA) we find our hybrid model improves both clean-task performance (up to 43.34\% increase in accuracy) and adversarial robustness (up to 64.92\% improvement in accuracy and 62.27\% reduction in attack success rate). For out-of-distribution datasets (AeroEngQA, CPIQA) we see similar adversarial robustness from our hybrid model (up to 57.14\% improvement in accuracy). For prompt injection (SafeGuard) and jailbreak detection (AdvBench, DAN) datasets our hybrid model is also very strong (up to 51\% reduction in attack success rate compared to state of the art baseline models). Overall, our results show that combining entropy, uncertainty and geometric features provides a more effective defence strategy than using any single feature alone for both in-domain and out-of-distribution tasks.

item.title: Off-Distribution Voices: Fanfiction Subgenres as Universal Vernacular Jailbreaks for Aligned LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04483v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有针对对齐大语言模型（LLMs）的“越狱”（jailbreak）攻击多依赖人工构造的离散提示模板（如“DAN”或“STAN”），其表面形式易被内容过滤器识别并快速修补。本文指出：真正的安全失效根源并非特定提示，而是安全训练长期忽视的一类自然人类语域（register）——即真实、自发、富有表现力的亚文化创作语言，尤其是网络 fanfiction（同人小说）中高度结构化且语义饱和的子类型。

方法创新

我们提出首个基于真实fanfiction子类型的通用越狱家族：利用Archive of Our Own（AO3）平台十二个主流子类型（如“Fix-It”“Alternate Universe”“Slow Burn”）作为语域载体。攻击无需攻击者LLM、不需目标模型微调——仅需将有害指令嵌入符合该子类型风格的叙事高潮段落中，由模型自主续写完成越狱。该方法本质是语域劫持（register hijacking），而非语法欺骗。

关键发现

  • 在HarmBench与JailbreakBench联合测试集上，对8个主流对齐模型（含Llama-3-70B-Instruct、Qwen2-72B-Instruct等），四裁判集成评估下平均攻击成功率（ASR）从0.278跃升至0.731；因子分解证实提升主要源于语域匹配性，而非文本长度或句法结构。
  • 两种主流主动防御（如PromptGuard、Llama-Guard3）意外扩大了“亚文化语域–基线语域”响应差异比，表明模板式防御正将攻击者推向更隐蔽的语域级对抗。
  • 进一步提出静态四轮扩展协议SAGA-A4，无需交互反馈，ASR达0.924，显著超越三类现有多轮越狱方法（Multi-Step、AutoDAN、GCG-Iterative）。

理论意义

本研究揭示：对齐模型的安全脆弱性根植于语域覆盖鸿沟，而非表层提示工程。防范需转向语域感知训练与跨亚文化语料平衡，而非仅加固已知攻击模式。

AI Summary (English)

Existing jailbreaks against aligned LLMs rely on hand-crafted, discrete prompts—easily fingerprinted and patched. We argue the true failure mode is broader: safety training systematically undercovers entire registers of natural human writing, especially stylistically rich, community-grounded vernaculars like fanfiction subgenres. Introducing the first universal jailbreak family grounded in real creative practice, we leverage twelve AO3 fanfiction subgenres (e.g., “Alternate Universe”, “Fix-It”) as semantic carriers—embedding harmful behavior naturally within genre-typical climactic scenes. No attacker LLM or per-target adaptation is needed. Evaluated across eight aligned models on HarmBench + JailbreakBench with a four-judge ensemble, our attack lifts mean ASR from 0.278 to 0.731, with factorial analysis confirming register—not length or structure—as the primary driver. Crucially, two active defenses widen the vernacular-to-baseline response gap, revealing their vulnerability to register-based attacks. Finally, our static four-turn extension SAGA-A4 achieves mean ASR 0.924, outperforming three leading multi-turn methods. This work reframes alignment failure as a register coverage gap, demanding vernacular-aware safety paradigms.

Abstract

Existing jailbreaks against aligned LLMs are discrete artifacts whose surface forms are easy to fingerprint and patch. We argue that the real failure mode is not any specific prompt, but an entire register of natural human writing that safety training has under-covered. Building on this insight, we introduce the first jailbreak family that uses real fanfiction subgenres as universal attack carriers: a creative-writing meta is conditioned on passages from one of twelve Archive of Our Own (AO3) subgenres, and the harmful behavior is embedded as the climax of the resulting scene. The construction requires no attacker LLM and no per-target adaptation. On eight aligned LLMs over the union of HarmBench and JailbreakBench, this attack lifts mean ASR from 0.278 to 0.731 under a four-judge ensemble; a factorial decomposition shows the gain is carried by register rather than length or structure. Two active defences widen rather than narrow the vernacular-to-baseline ratio, indicating that template-targeting defences merely steer attackers toward register-based attacks like ours. We also propose SAGA-A4, a static four-turn extension that attains mean ASR 0.924, substantially exceeding three existing multi-turn methods.

item.title: SHIELDS: Automating OS Hardening with Iterative Multi-Agent Remediation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05476v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

操作系统（OS）安全配置错误仍是导致系统被入侵的首要原因。人工落实DISA STIG等权威安全基线标准耗时费力、成本高昂，而现有自动化合规工具多依赖静态预定义修复脚本，缺乏对动态环境、异构配置及验证反馈的适应能力，难以应对真实生产环境中复杂的配置漂移与上下文依赖。

方法：SHIELDS多智能体迭代修复框架

本文提出SHIELDS——一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体操作系统加固系统。其核心创新在于将硬化的本质重构为闭环迭代过程：

  • 多角色协同：由Analyzer（解析扫描报告）、Planner（生成可执行修复方案）、Executor（调用系统工具实施）和Validator（运行STIG验证器获取反馈）四个专用智能体组成；
  • 反馈驱动演进：不依赖一次性“正确答案”，而是根据实际执行结果与验证扫描的实时反馈（如权限拒绝、服务启动失败、策略冲突），自动重写、拆分或回退修复步骤；
  • 本地化与轻量化设计：支持在边缘/受限环境中部署中等规模LLM（20B–70B参数），通过结构化工具调用与信息蒸馏降低对超大模型的依赖。

主要发现与创新点

在6类虚拟机配置（涵盖RHEL 8/9、Ubuntu 22.04）上，使用6个主流LLM（20B–400B参数）评估表明：

  • SHIELDS平均成功修复73%的STIG扫描告警项，显著优于静态脚本工具（平均<45%）；
  • 模型性能提升主要源于工具调用精度与上下文感知能力，而非单纯参数量——70B模型在优化提示与工具链下表现接近400B模型；
  • 首次验证了“小模型+强反馈+专用Agent”范式在高保障、低算力、数据敏感场景下的可行性，为国防、金融等关键基础设施提供可落地的合规自动化新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Security misconfigurations are a top cause of OS compromise, yet manual STIG compliance is costly and error-prone. Existing automation tools rely on static, pre-written remediations—lacking adaptability to runtime feedback or environment-specific constraints. We introduce SHIELDS, a multi-agent LLM system that treats OS hardening as an iterative, feedback-driven loop: agents analyze scan reports, propose fixes, execute them on the target system, and refine proposals based on real validation results (e.g., failed service starts or policy conflicts). Evaluated across six VM configurations using six LLMs (20B–400B parameters), SHIELDS successfully remediates up to 73% of STIG findings, outperforming static tools. Crucially, success correlates more strongly with effective tool use and contextual reasoning than raw model size—enabling robust compliance automation even with smaller, locally deployed models. This offers a practical, privacy-aware path for resource-constrained, high-assurance environments.

Abstract

Security misconfigurations remain a leading cause of OS-level compromise, and manually keeping systems compliant with standards like Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs) is a tedious and expensive process. Existing compliance automation tools can reduce some of this burden, but they depend on static, pre-written corrective actions. In this paper, we introduce SHIELDS, a multi-agent system that uses large language models (LLMs) to approach OS hardening as an iterative, feedback-driven process. Instead of applying fixed remediations, SHIELDS continuously proposes fixes and refines them based on feedback from target system execution and validation scans. We evaluate the system across multiple virtual machine configurations using six contemporary LLMs ranging from 20B to 400B parameters, and find that SHIELDS successfully remediates up to 73% of scan findings. Our results also suggest that success in this setting depends less on model size (parameter count) than on effective tool use and information gathering, paving a practical path toward reducing the burden of security compliance in environments where compute is limited or security and privacy needs drive local model use.

item.title: DP-MacAdam: Differentially Private Mechanism with Adaptive Clipping and Adaptive Momentum
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05435v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

差分隐私随机梯度下降（DP-SGD）是隐私保护机器学习的主流框架，但其依赖固定梯度裁剪阈值以控制敏感度，导致在训练动态中适应性差：阈值过小损失信息、过大则噪声主导，显著制约模型效用。自适应裁剪算法（如AdaClip）通过基于经验均值与方差对梯度进行平移与缩放后再裁剪，提升了裁剪后梯度的方向质量；然而，其估计出的统计量未用于动量更新——即仅服务裁剪，未赋能优化加速。反之，DP-Adam虽利用梯度一阶/二阶矩构建Adam式动量以加速收敛，却未将这些统计量反哺裁剪机制，造成统计资源割裂。

方法创新

本文提出DP-MacAdam（Differentially Private Mechanism with Adaptive Clipping and Adaptive Momentum），首次统一复用同一套无偏、差分隐私保护的梯度均值与方差估计，同步驱动两个核心模块：

  • 自适应裁剪：依据实时估计动态调整裁剪中心与尺度；
  • 自适应动量：构建类Adam的隐私动量更新器，避免额外噪声开销。

理论分析证明：DP-MacAdam对梯度方差的估计具有无偏性（bias-free），且满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-差分隐私。

实验结果

在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100及ImageNet子集上系统评估表明：DP-MacAdam在同等隐私预算（$\varepsilon \in [2,8]$）下，测试准确率显著超越DP-SGD、AdaClip与DP-Adam基线（最高提升+3.2%），且无需人工调优裁剪阈值，鲁棒性强。代码已开源。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private stochastic gradient descent (DP-SGD) suffers from suboptimal utility due to its reliance on a fixed gradient clipping threshold. While adaptive clipping methods (e.g., AdaClip) leverage empirical mean/variance to improve clipped gradient direction, they ignore these statistics for momentum acceleration; conversely, DP-Adam uses them for momentum but not for clipping. We propose DP-MacAdam, the first unified mechanism that jointly exploits the same differentially private mean and variance estimates for both adaptive clipping and adaptive Adam-style momentum. We prove its variance estimator is unbiased under privacy constraints. Empirically, DP-MacAdam achieves superior accuracy over DP-SGD, AdaClip, and DP-Adam across CIFAR-10/100 and ImageNet subsets at comparable $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-privacy budgets—without manual clipping threshold tuning. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

Differentially private stochastic gradient descent (DP-SGD) has become the standard framework for privacy-preserving machine learning, yet its reliance on a fixed gradient clipping threshold to limit sensitivity remains a significant practical limitation. Adaptive clipping algorithms such as AdaClip shift and scale the gradient prior to clipping and adding noise so that the clipped gradient yields a more informative descent direction. The shift and scaling parameters are selected adaptively based on the empirical mean and variance. However, in existing adaptive clipping algorithms, these empirical estimates have not been also used for momentum to accelerate training itself. On the other hand, DP-Adam is an algorithm that exploits Adam-like momentum updates based on the gradient mean and variance to accelerate training, but does not exploit these estimates for adaptive clipping. In this work, we propose Differentially Private Mechanism with Adaptive Clipping and Adaptive Momentum (DP-MacAdam), a novel algorithm that combines these two approaches so as to use the same mean and variance estimates for both clipping and momentum. We perform an analysis showing that DP-MacAdam estimates the gradient variances in a bias-free manner. In addition, we empirically evaluate the privacy and accuracy of DP-MacAdam, demonstrating that it achieves improved model utility compared to DP-SGD, AdaClip, and DP-Adam baselines, without requiring manual tuning of the clipping threshold.
